('structrace__type', 'race_type').name: race_type
('structrace__type', 'race_type').brief: Represents the characteristics and abilities of a race in the game.
('structrace__type', 'race_type').details: This struct encapsulates the various attributes and capabilities associated with a race in the game. It includes the race's name, whether it can be selected by player characters (PCs), and several sets of flags that define the race's actions, afflictions, offensive capabilities, immunities, resistances, vulnerabilities, default form, and body parts. The 'name' field is a case-insensitive string representing the race's name. The 'pc_race' boolean indicates if the race is available for player selection. The 'act', 'aff', 'off', 'imm', 'res', 'vuln', 'form', and 'parts' fields are instances of the Flags class, each representing different aspects of the race's behavior and traits, such as actions it can perform, afflictions it can suffer, offensive and defensive capabilities, and its physical form and body parts.

('class_room_i_d', 'RoomID').name: RoomID
('class_room_i_d', 'RoomID').brief: Encapsulates a room identifier with validation and serialization capabilities.
('class_room_i_d', 'RoomID').details: The RoomID class represents a unique identifier for a room, encapsulating both a room number and a virtual number (vnum). It provides functionality to check the validity of the RoomID, serialize it to an integer, and retrieve its components. The class supports construction from a Vnum object or a String, and offers comparison operators for sorting and equality checks. The RoomID can be converted to a string representation, and it maintains a consistent internal state with a default invalid state upon construction. The class ensures that the room number and vnum are correctly serialized into a single integer, with specific bits reserved for each component.

('group___body_part_flags', 'BodyPartFla.name: BodyPartConstants
('group___body_part_flags', 'BodyPartFla.brief: Constants representing various body parts and combat-related attributes for mobs.
('group___body_part_flags', 'BodyPartFla.details: These constants define the physical body parts and combat-related features of mobs in the game.

('class_departed_player', 'DepartedPlaye.name: DepartedPlayer
('class_departed_player', 'DepartedPlaye.brief: Represents a player who has departed from a game or session.
('class_departed_player', 'DepartedPlaye.details: The DepartedPlayer class is used to manage players who have left a game or session. It stores the player's name and maintains pointers to the next and previous departed players, allowing for the creation of a linked list of departed players. The class provides a default constructor and a virtual destructor, ensuring proper cleanup in derived classes. Copy construction and assignment are disabled to prevent unintended copying of player instances.

('group___command_group_shorthand_flags'.name: CommandGroupShorthandFlags
('group___command_group_shorthand_flags'.brief: Defines shorthand constants for various group flags used in the game.

('structaffect_1_1table__entry', 'affect.name: affect::table_entry
('structaffect_1_1table__entry', 'affect.brief: Encapsulates messages related to the expiration of a skill.
('structaffect_1_1table__entry', 'affect.details: This struct is used to store various messages associated with a skill when it wears off. It includes the name of the skill, a message for the user indicating the skill has worn off, a message for the room to notify others present, and a message for objects that may be affected by the skill's expiration.

('class_exit_prototype', 'ExitPrototype'.name: ExitPrototype
('class_exit_prototype', 'ExitPrototype'.brief: Manages the prototype for an exit in a virtual environment.
('class_exit_prototype', 'ExitPrototype'.details: The ExitPrototype class is responsible for defining the initial state and properties of an exit within a virtual environment. It includes a description and keyword for the exit, a target virtual number (Vnum) indicating where the exit leads, and a set of flags that determine the exit's behavior. Additionally, it manages a key, represented as a Vnum, which is used to unlock the exit, with -1 serving as a placeholder value. The class provides a constructor for initializing these properties from a file stream, and it prevents copying and assignment to ensure unique instances of exit prototypes.

('group___generic_object_types', 'Generi.name: GenericObjectTypes
('group___generic_object_types', 'Generi.brief: Constants representing generic object types for creation.
('group___generic_object_types', 'Generi.details: These constants define the types of generic objects that can be created in the game.

('structc_j_s_o_n', 'cJSON').name: cJSON
('structc_j_s_o_n', 'cJSON').brief: Represents a JSON item in a tree structure.
('structc_j_s_o_n', 'cJSON').details: The cJSON struct is used to represent a JSON item, which can be part of a JSON array or object. It contains pointers for navigating through sibling items (next and prev) and child items (child), allowing traversal of complex JSON structures. The type field indicates the type of the JSON item, such as string or number. Depending on the type, the item may store a string value (valuestring), an integer value (valueint), or a double value (valuedouble). The string field holds the name of the item if it is part of an object, facilitating key-value pair representation in JSON objects.

('group___note_file_paths', 'NoteFilePat.name: NoteFileConstants
('group___note_file_paths', 'NoteFilePat.brief: Constants representing file paths for various note categories.

('group___exit_flags', 'ExitFlags').name: ExitFlags
('group___exit_flags', 'ExitFlags').brief: Constants representing attributes and behaviors for room exits.
('group___exit_flags', 'ExitFlags').details: These flags define the properties of exits in rooms, such as whether they are doors, locked, or passable.

('class_flags', 'Flags').name: Flags
('class_flags', 'Flags').brief: A class for managing and manipulating a set of bit flags.
('class_flags', 'Flags').details: The Flags class provides a mechanism to manage a collection of bit flags, represented by the nested enumeration Bit. Each flag corresponds to a specific bit position, allowing for efficient storage and manipulation of multiple flags using bitwise operations. The class supports various constructors for initializing flags from different types, including unsigned long, Bit, C-style strings, and the custom String class. It offers a range of operators for combining, removing, and toggling flags, as well as methods to check for the presence of specific flags or combinations thereof. The Flags class also includes utility functions to convert the flags to an unsigned long or a String representation. This class is particularly useful in scenarios where multiple boolean options need to be managed compactly and efficiently.

('class_vnum', 'Vnum').name: Vnum
('class_vnum', 'Vnum').brief: Encapsulates a virtual number (vnum) with comparison operators.
('class_vnum', 'Vnum').details: The Vnum class represents a virtual number, internally stored as a signed long. It is designed to be initialized with an unsigned long, ensuring the top bit is always 0. This design allows for potential future use of the top bits for additional purposes, such as room identifiers, while reserving the very top bit. The class provides a constructor, copy constructor, assignment operator, and a destructor. It also includes a method to retrieve the value and implements comparison operators for ordering and equality checks.

('group___area_types', 'AreaTypes').name: AreaTypes
('group___area_types', 'AreaTypes').brief: Constants representing types of areas in the game.
('group___area_types', 'AreaTypes').details: These constants define the classification of areas based on their purpose or accessibility.

('class_mob_prog_act_list', 'MobProgActL.name: MobProgActList
('class_mob_prog_act_list', 'MobProgActL.brief: Encapsulates a linked list node for mobile program actions in a game.
('class_mob_prog_act_list', 'MobProgActL.details: The MobProgActList class represents a node in a linked list used to manage actions for mobile programs within a game environment. Each node contains a buffer string, a character pointer, an object pointer, and a generic void pointer for additional data. The class provides a default constructor and a virtual destructor, ensuring proper cleanup in derived classes. Copy constructor and assignment operator are private to prevent copying of list nodes, maintaining the integrity of the linked list structure.

('structdeity__type', 'deity_type').name: deity_type
('structdeity__type', 'deity_type').brief: Represents an entity with a name, alignment, and a numerical value.
('structdeity__type', 'deity_type').details: This struct encapsulates an entity characterized by a name and alignment, both represented as Strings, and an integer value. The String class provides extended functionality over std::string, including case-insensitive comparisons, which can be useful for handling the name and alignment properties. The integer value can be used to represent any numerical attribute associated with the entity, such as a score, level, or identifier.

('classaffect_1_1_affect', 'affect::Affe.name: affect::Affect
('classaffect_1_1_affect', 'affect::Affe.brief: Represents an affect with various attributes and behaviors.
('classaffect_1_1_affect', 'affect::Affe.details: The Affect class encapsulates the concept of an affect, which includes various attributes such as type, level, duration, and modifiers. It is designed to be part of a doubly linked list, with pointers to the next and previous affects. The class includes fields that are critical for computing checksums, ensuring data integrity when these attributes change. The bitvector field is used to store flags, which can be converted to and from a Flags object. The class is packed and aligned to avoid padding, which is important for checksum calculations.

('structflag__type', 'flag_type').name: flag_type
('structflag__type', 'flag_type').brief: Represents a configuration option with a name, associated bit flag, and settable status.
('structflag__type', 'flag_type').details: This struct encapsulates a configuration option that includes a name, a bit flag from the Flags::Bit enumeration, and a boolean indicating whether the option can be set. The 'name' field is a string representing the name of the option. The 'bit' field is an enumeration value that specifies the bit flag associated with this option, allowing for bitwise operations. The 'settable' field is a boolean that indicates if the option can be modified, providing a mechanism to control which options are user-configurable.

('structint__app__type', 'int_app_type').name: int_app_type
('structint__app__type', 'int_app_type').brief: Represents a character's learning ability and mana bonus.
('structint__app__type', 'int_app_type').details: This struct encapsulates attributes related to a character's learning ability and mana. The 'learn' field indicates the character's ability to learn new skills or spells, while the 'manap' field provides a bonus to the character's mana, as introduced by Montrey. These attributes are typically used in role-playing games to manage character progression and resource management.

('group___extra_item_flags', 'ExtraItemF.name: ExtraItemFlags
('group___extra_item_flags', 'ExtraItemF.brief: Constants representing additional attributes for items.
('group___extra_item_flags', 'ExtraItemF.details: These flags define special properties and behaviors for items in the game.

('class_war_1_1_event', 'War::Event').name: War::Event
('class_war_1_1_event', 'War::Event').brief: Encapsulates event data and provides a linked list structure.
('class_war_1_1_event', 'War::Event').details: The Event class represents an event with various attributes such as type, two string fields, a number, and a timestamp. It is designed to be part of a linked list, with a pointer to the next Event object. The class includes a default constructor and a virtual destructor, ensuring proper cleanup in derived classes. Copy construction and assignment are disabled to prevent unintended copying of Event objects.

('structwiznet__type', 'wiznet_type').name: wiznet_type
('structwiznet__type', 'wiznet_type').brief: Represents an entity with a name, flag, level, and description.
('structwiznet__type', 'wiznet_type').details: This struct encapsulates an entity characterized by a name, a flag indicating specific attributes or states using the Flags::Bit enumeration, a level representing the entity's hierarchy or importance, and a description providing additional context or information. The name and description are managed using the String class, which offers extended functionality over std::string, including case-insensitive comparisons.

('group___mobile_offensive_flags', 'Mobi.name: MobileOffensiveFlags
('group___mobile_offensive_flags', 'Mobi.brief: Constants representing offensive abilities and assist behaviors for mobiles.
('group___mobile_offensive_flags', 'Mobi.details: These flags define various offensive actions and assist behaviors that mobiles can perform in the game.

('group___pulse_constants', 'PulseConsta.name: PulseConstants
('group___pulse_constants', 'PulseConsta.brief: Pulse and timing constants for game events and timers.

('group___mobile_affect_flags', 'MobileA.name: AffectBitsConstants
('group___mobile_affect_flags', 'MobileA.brief: Constants representing various affect flags for mobs and characters.
('group___mobile_affect_flags', 'MobileA.details: These constants define the effects and statuses that can be applied to mobs and characters in the game.

('structconn_1_1_get_guild_state', 'conn.name: conn::GetGuildState
('structconn_1_1_get_guild_state', 'conn.brief: Represents a state for handling guild-related input.
('structconn_1_1_get_guild_state', 'conn.details: The GetGuildState struct is a specialized state within the state machine that processes input related to guild operations. It inherits from the State class and overrides the handleInput method to manage transitions based on guild-related input. This struct is used to interpret input from a Descriptor object and determine the appropriate next state based on the guild information provided in the input string.

('class_social', 'Social').name: Social
('class_social', 'Social').brief: Encapsulates the data and behavior for a social interaction in a game.
('class_social', 'Social').details: The Social class represents a social interaction within a game, storing various string messages that describe how the interaction is perceived by different participants. It includes messages for when no target is specified, when a target is found, and when the interaction is automatic. The class also supports linking to previous and next Social objects, allowing for a sequence of interactions. Copy construction and assignment are disabled to prevent unintended copying of Social objects.

('class_player', 'Player').name: Player
('class_player', 'Player').brief: Encapsulates player-specific data and attributes for a character in the game.
('class_player', 'Player').details: The Player class manages data and attributes that are unique to player characters (PCs) in the game. It maintains a reference to the associated Character object and includes various properties such as player identifiers, strings for in-game messages, timestamps for last actions, and counters for experience and kills. The class also handles quest-related data, including quest givers, objectives, and locations. Additionally, it manages skill points, group knowledge, roleplay points, and player flags. The Player class supports customization through aliases, color settings, and additional flags for player status and video settings. It also tracks relationships with other characters, such as familiars and undead minions, and maintains a list of warp locations. The class is designed to be non-copyable and non-assignable to ensure unique instances for each player character.

('structconn_1_1_get_alignment_state', '.name: conn::GetAlignmentState
('structconn_1_1_get_alignment_state', '.brief: Represents a state in the connection handling process that manages alignment-related interactions.
('structconn_1_1_get_alignment_state', '.details: The GetAlignmentState struct inherits from the State class and encapsulates the behavior associated with obtaining or processing a character's alignment information within the connection state machine. Its handleInput method processes input received from a Descriptor, potentially updating or retrieving alignment data, and transitions to the next appropriate state based on the input. This state is part of a larger state-driven system managing network connection interactions, specifically focusing on alignment-related functionalities.

('structskill_1_1skill__table__t', 'skil.name: skill::skill_table_t
('structskill_1_1skill__table__t', 'skil.brief: Encapsulates the attributes and requirements for skills and spells in the game.
('structskill_1_1skill__table__t', 'skil.details: This struct defines the properties and requirements for skills, including spells, within the game. Each skill has a name, and its effectiveness and accessibility are determined by various factors such as the skill level required by different guilds, the difficulty rating for learning, and the minimum mana needed for usage. Additionally, it specifies the legal targets for the skill, the caster's or user's required position, and the waiting time after using the skill. For spells, a function pointer is provided to define the spell's behavior. The struct also includes messages for damage caused by the skill and specifies any remort guild requirements. Furthermore, it outlines the evolution costs for guilds to progress to higher levels of skill mastery.

('class_exit', 'Exit').name: Exit
('class_exit', 'Exit').brief: Manages an exit in a virtual environment, linking rooms and handling directional logic.
('class_exit', 'Exit').details: The Exit class represents a connection between rooms in a virtual environment, using an ExitPrototype to define its initial state. It maintains a set of flags to manage its current state, which can reset to the prototype's values during area resets. The class provides static methods to retrieve the name of a direction and its reverse, facilitating navigation logic. It also includes methods to access the exit's key, keyword, and description, which are essential for interaction within the environment. The Exit class ensures that each exit is linked to a target room, allowing for seamless transitions between different areas.

('class_tail', 'Tail').name: Tail
('class_tail', 'Tail').brief: A class representing a linked list node with additional metadata.
('class_tail', 'Tail').details: The Tail class encapsulates a node in a linked list structure, where each node contains a pointer to a Character object, a name represented by a String, a set of Flags for additional metadata, and a pointer to the next Tail node in the list. It provides a default constructor and a virtual destructor, ensuring proper cleanup in derived classes. The copy constructor and assignment operator are private, preventing copying of Tail instances.

('group___damage_type_constants', 'Damag.name: DamageTypeConstants
('group___damage_type_constants', 'Damag.brief: Constants representing various types of damage in the game.
('group___damage_type_constants', 'Damag.details: These constants define the damage classes used for attacks, spells, and other effects.

('structraffects', 'raffects').name: raffects
('structraffects', 'raffects').brief: Encapsulates properties and behaviors for a configurable entity with identification and flag management.
('structraffects', 'raffects').details: This struct represents a configurable entity characterized by a description, a short name, a group identifier, a unique ID, and a chance value. It includes a Flags object to manage additional properties or behaviors through bit flags. The 'description' and 'shortname' fields use the String class, allowing for extended string manipulation and case-insensitive comparisons. The 'group' and 'id' fields are integers used to categorize and uniquely identify the entity, respectively. The 'chance' field represents a probability or likelihood value, and the 'add' field is a Flags object that manages a set of bit flags associated with the entity.

('group___dice_constants', 'DiceConstant.name: DiceConstants
('group___dice_constants', 'DiceConstant.brief: Constants representing dice attributes for rolls.
('group___dice_constants', 'DiceConstant.details: These constants define the attributes of dice rolls used in the game mechanics.

('structconn_1_1_get_weapon_state', 'con.name: conn::GetWeaponState
('structconn_1_1_get_weapon_state', 'con.brief: Represents a state for handling weapon-related input in a network connection.
('structconn_1_1_get_weapon_state', 'con.details: The GetWeaponState struct is a specialized state within a state machine that processes input related to weapon selection or management in a networked environment. It inherits from the State class and overrides the handleInput method to interpret input data, provided through a Descriptor object and a String reference, to transition to an appropriate new state based on the input. This struct is part of a larger framework managing network connections and user interactions, specifically focusing on weapon-related actions.

('structgem_1_1quality__st', 'gem::quali.name: gem::quality_st
('structgem_1_1quality__st', 'gem::quali.brief: Represents a keyword with associated quality and level metrics.
('structgem_1_1quality__st', 'gem::quali.details: This struct is used to store information about a keyword, including its quality and level. The 'keyword' field is a pointer to a character array representing the keyword itself. The 'quality' field is an integer that indicates the quality metric of the keyword, which could be used for ranking or evaluation purposes. The 'level' field is an integer that represents the level or depth associated with the keyword, which might be used in hierarchical or categorical contexts.

('structspec__type', 'spec_type').name: spec_type
('structspec__type', 'spec_type').brief: Encapsulates a special function with its name and pointer to the function.
('structspec__type', 'spec_type').details: This struct represents a special function by storing its name as a String and a pointer to the function itself. The 'name' field holds the name of the special function, allowing for easy identification and retrieval. The 'function' field is a pointer to the actual function, enabling the execution of the special function when needed. This struct is useful in scenarios where functions need to be dynamically referenced and invoked by name.

('group___remort_affect_types', 'RemortA.name: RemortAffectConstants
('group___remort_affect_types', 'RemortA.brief: Constants for remort system affects, including beneficial and detrimental effects.

('structchr__app__type', 'chr_app_type').name: chr_app_type
('structchr__app__type', 'chr_app_type').brief: Encapsulates a probability value as an integer.
('structchr__app__type', 'chr_app_type').details: This struct represents a chance or probability value, stored as an integer. The 'chance' member can be used to indicate the likelihood of an event occurring, typically expressed as a percentage or a ratio. It is important to ensure that the value of 'chance' is within a valid range, such as 0 to 100 if representing a percentage.

('structsex__type', 'sex_type').name: sex_type
('structsex__type', 'sex_type').brief: Encapsulates a name using the extended String class.
('structsex__type', 'sex_type').details: This struct holds a single member, 'name', which is of type String. The String class extends std::string to provide additional functionality, including case-insensitive comparisons. This struct can be used to store and manipulate names with enhanced string operations.

('group___communication_flags', 'Communi.name: CommunicationFlags
('group___communication_flags', 'Communi.brief: Constants representing communication preferences and restrictions.
('group___communication_flags', 'Communi.details: These flags define the communication settings for players and mobs, such as muting channels or enabling compact mode.

('class_auction', 'Auction').name: Auction
('class_auction', 'Auction').brief: Represents an auction process within the game, managing items, participants, and bidding state.
('class_auction', 'Auction').details: The Auction class encapsulates the state and behavior of an in-game auction, including the item being auctioned, the seller and buyer participants, current bid amount, auction status, countdown pulses, and minimum bid. It provides methods to initialize and update the auction state, as well as to check if a given object or character is participating in the auction. The class manages its internal state privately, preventing copying or assignment to ensure integrity of auction data.

('class_battle', 'Battle').name: Battle
('class_battle', 'Battle').brief: Encapsulates the state and behavior of a battle scenario.
('class_battle', 'Battle').details: The Battle class represents a battle scenario with configurable parameters such as low, high, and fee. It also tracks the state of the battle with flags like issued and start. The class provides a default constructor and a virtual destructor, ensuring proper cleanup in derived classes. Copy construction and assignment are disabled to prevent unintended copying of battle instances.

('group___loot_weapon_vnums', 'LootWeapo.name: WeaponTypes
('group___loot_weapon_vnums', 'LootWeapo.brief: Constants representing special properties of weapons.

These flags define unique attributes and effects that weapons can have in the game.
Vnums are objects in vegita.are

('structliq__type', 'liq_type').name: liq_type
('structliq__type', 'liq_type').brief: Represents a type of liquid with specific properties.
('structliq__type', 'liq_type').details: The Liquid struct encapsulates the properties of a liquid, including its name, color, and an array of effects. The name and color are represented using the String class, which allows for versatile string manipulation and case-insensitive comparisons. The affect array holds five integer values that describe various effects or attributes associated with the liquid.

('group___damage_type_immunity_flags', '.name: DamageTypeImmunities
('group___damage_type_immunity_flags', '.brief: Constants representing immunity to various types of damage.
('group___damage_type_immunity_flags', '.details: These constants define the types of damage that mobs can be immune to in the game.

('structconn_1_1_read_m_o_t_d_state', 'c.name: conn::ReadMOTDState
('structconn_1_1_read_m_o_t_d_state', 'c.brief: Represents the state for reading the Message of the Day (MOTD) in a network connection.
('structconn_1_1_read_m_o_t_d_state', 'c.details: The ReadMOTDState struct is a specialized state within a network connection's state machine, responsible for handling the input related to reading the Message of the Day (MOTD). It inherits from the State class and overrides the handleInput method to process input specific to the MOTD. This method takes a Descriptor pointer, which provides context or data for processing the input, and a constant reference to a String object representing the input to be handled. The handleInput method returns a pointer to a new State object, indicating the next state after processing the input.

('class_mob_prog', 'MobProg').name: MobProg
('class_mob_prog', 'MobProg').brief: Encapsulates a mobile program with its type, argument list, and command list.
('class_mob_prog', 'MobProg').details: The MobProg class represents a mobile program, which includes a type, an argument list, and a command list. It provides functionality to convert between string representations of mob program types and their corresponding Flags::Bit enumeration values. The constructor initializes a MobProg object by reading from a file stream, allowing the program to be constructed with data from a file. The class also includes private copy constructor and assignment operator to prevent copying of MobProg objects.

('class_mobile_prototype', 'MobileProtot.name: MobilePrototype
('class_mobile_prototype', 'MobileProtot.brief: Represents a prototype for a mobile entity in a game environment.
('class_mobile_prototype', 'MobileProtot.details: The MobilePrototype class encapsulates the in-memory representation of a mobile entity, commonly referred to as a 'mob', in a game environment. It is associated with a specific area and virtual number (vnum) and contains various attributes that define the mob's characteristics and behaviors. These attributes include descriptive strings, flags for actions and effects, combat statistics, and other properties such as alignment, level, and wealth. The class also manages mobile programs (mobprogs) which dictate scripted behaviors for the mob. The MobilePrototype class provides functionality to read and process these mobile programs from a file. It is designed to be used within a larger game system where mobs are dynamically created and managed based on their prototypes.

('structcsetting__type', 'csetting_type'.name: csetting_type
('structcsetting__type', 'csetting_type'.brief: Represents a text style with color, boldness, and a name.
('structcsetting__type', 'csetting_type'.details: This struct encapsulates the styling attributes for text, including an integer representing the color, a boolean-like integer indicating boldness, and a String for the name of the style. It allows for easy management and application of text styles in a program, with the added benefit of case-insensitive name comparisons due to the extended String class.

('classutil_1_1_image', 'util::Image').name: util::Image
('classutil_1_1_image', 'util::Image').brief: Encapsulates image data and operations for manipulating image dimensions and pixel values.
('classutil_1_1_image', 'util::Image').details: The Image class provides functionality to manage and manipulate image data, including retrieving and setting image dimensions, accessing pixel values by color channel, and loading image data from a file. It defines an enumeration for color channels (red, green, blue, alpha) and provides methods to get the width and height of the image. The class ensures proper resource management by disallowing copy construction and assignment. It also includes private methods to set the image dimensions, which are used internally to manage the image's state.

('group___weapon_class_constants', 'Weap.name: WeaponClassConstants
('group___weapon_class_constants', 'Weap.brief: Constants representing various weapon classes in the game.
('group___weapon_class_constants', 'Weap.details: These constants define the types of weapons that can be used by characters and mobs.

('group___game_parameters', 'GameParamet.name: GameParameters
('group___game_parameters', 'GameParamet.brief: Constants representing various game parameters and settings.
('group___game_parameters', 'GameParamet.details: These constants define the limits, levels, and other configurations for the game.

('structstr__app__type', 'str_app_type').name: str_app_type
('structstr__app__type', 'str_app_type').brief: Encapsulates attribute bonuses for a character.
('structstr__app__type', 'str_app_type').details: This struct defines various attribute bonuses that can be applied to a character in a game. It includes bonuses to hit (tohit), damage (todam), carrying capacity (carry), wielding ability (wield), and stamina (stp). These bonuses are used to enhance a character's performance in different aspects of gameplay.

('structweapon__table__t', 'weapon_table.name: weapon_table_t
('structweapon__table__t', 'weapon_table.brief: Represents a skill or spell with associated metadata.
('structweapon__table__t', 'weapon_table.details: This struct encapsulates the properties of a skill or spell, including its name, a unique virtual number (vnum), a type identifier, and its classification within the skill::type enumeration. It is used to define and manage skills or spells within the application, allowing for easy access to their attributes and facilitating operations such as lookups and comparisons.

('group___room_vnums', 'RoomVnums').name: RoomVirtualNumbers
('group___room_vnums', 'RoomVnums').brief: Constants representing well-known room virtual numbers.
('group___room_vnums', 'RoomVnums').details: These constants define specific room types and their virtual numbers used in the game.

('classworldmap_1_1_coordinate', 'worldm.name: worldmap::Coordinate
('classworldmap_1_1_coordinate', 'worldm.brief: Encapsulates a 2D coordinate with serialization and validation capabilities.
('classworldmap_1_1_coordinate', 'worldm.details: The Coordinate class represents a two-dimensional point with x and y integer values. It provides multiple constructors for initialization, including default, parameterized, integer deserialization, and string parsing. The class ensures that coordinates are valid by checking that x and y are non-negative and fit within a 15-bit representation. It offers methods to serialize the coordinate to an integer and to convert it to a string representation. The class also supports equality and inequality comparisons and includes a method to check the validity of the coordinate. The private method num_bits_coord returns the number of bits used for coordinate representation, which is 15.

('structconn_1_1_get_race_state', 'conn:.name: conn::GetRaceState
('structconn_1_1_get_race_state', 'conn:.brief: Represents a state responsible for handling user input related to race selection in a character creation or customization process.
('structconn_1_1_get_race_state', 'conn:.details: GetRaceState is a derived struct from State that manages the interaction when a user is prompted to select or input a race. Its handleInput method processes the user's input, interprets it as a race name or identifier, and performs the necessary lookup using race_lookup. Based on the input, it transitions to the next appropriate state or prompts the user again if the input is invalid. This struct facilitates the flow of race selection within the broader state machine managing character setup or modification.

('structset__gear', 'set_gear').name: set_gear
('structset__gear', 'set_gear').brief: Encapsulates information about a gear set and its bonuses.
('structset__gear', 'set_gear').details: The set_gear struct holds information about a specific gear set, including its unique identifier, display name, and bonuses for equipping different numbers of pieces from the set. The set_name is an integer representing the exact set name, such as SET_MAGE_INVOKER. The display field is a String that describes what is shown to the player. The set1 through set5 fields are Strings that describe the bonuses received for equipping 1 to 5 pieces of the gear set, respectively.

('struct_mag_t__type', 'MagT_type').name: MagT_type
('struct_mag_t__type', 'MagT_type').details: This struct holds two String objects, 'male' and 'female', which are used to store gender-specific string values. The String class extends std::string, providing additional functionality such as case-insensitive comparisons, making it suitable for handling gender-related text data in a flexible manner.

('class_extra_descr', 'ExtraDescr').name: ExtraDescr
('class_extra_descr', 'ExtraDescr').brief: Encapsulates additional descriptive data for a room or object.
('class_extra_descr', 'ExtraDescr').details: The ExtraDescr class is used to store extra descriptive information associated with a room or object in a game or simulation. It contains a keyword that triggers the description and the description itself, which provides additional details when examined. The class supports construction from keyword and description strings, copying from another ExtraDescr object, and maintains a linked list structure through the 'next' pointer. Assignment is disabled to prevent unintended copying.

('struct_vnum_range', 'VnumRange').name: VnumRange
('struct_vnum_range', 'VnumRange').brief: Encapsulates a range of virtual numbers (vnums) with comparison capabilities.
('struct_vnum_range', 'VnumRange').details: The VnumRange struct is designed to represent a range of virtual numbers, defined by a minimum (min) and maximum (max) Vnum. It provides a constructor to initialize these values and an overloaded less-than operator to facilitate comparisons between VnumRange objects. This operator allows for determining if one range is completely before another, based on the maximum of the first range being less than the minimum of the second range. This struct is particularly useful for organizing and searching within collections of vnum ranges, such as in an areas map.

('class_object_prototype', 'ObjectProtot.name: ObjectPrototype
('class_object_prototype', 'ObjectProtot.brief: Represents a prototype for game objects with attributes and behaviors.
('class_object_prototype', 'ObjectProtot.details: The ObjectPrototype class encapsulates the definition of a game object prototype, including its attributes and behaviors. It is associated with a specific area and identified by a virtual number (vnum). The class maintains various properties such as name, description, material, and type, as well as flags for extra and wear attributes. It also supports additional descriptive data through ExtraDescr and can be affected by various affects, tracked by an affect checksum for consistency. The class manages object values, level, condition, weight, cost, and supports guild associations. It is designed to be non-copyable to ensure unique instances and is linked to other prototypes through a pointer to the next prototype in a list.

('structeq__roll__t', 'eq_roll_t').name: eq_roll_t
('structeq__roll__t', 'eq_roll_t').brief: Represents an equipment roll with a type and a virtual number.
('structeq__roll__t', 'eq_roll_t').details: The eq_roll_t struct is used to define an equipment roll in a game or simulation. It contains two integer fields: 'type', which specifies the category or classification of the equipment, and 'vnum', which stands for virtual number and uniquely identifies the equipment item within its category. This struct is typically used in contexts where equipment needs to be managed, identified, or categorized based on these attributes.

('class_object', 'Object').name: Object
('class_object', 'Object').brief: Encapsulates a game object with attributes, behaviors, and relationships to other entities.
('class_object', 'Object').details: The Object class represents a single game object within the game world, encapsulating various attributes and behaviors. It maintains a reference to its prototype (ObjectPrototype) and tracks its association with a reset (Reset). The class supports hierarchical relationships, allowing objects to be contained within other objects or rooms, and to be carried by characters. It includes attributes such as name, description, material, and owner, which are represented using the extended String class for additional functionality. The class manages extra descriptive data through ExtraDescr, and uses Flags to handle extra and wear flags. Object values are encapsulated using ObjectValue, supporting conversion and arithmetic operations. The class also manages affects, both permanent and gem-related, using affect::Affect, and provides mechanisms to update and deduplicate these affects. The identifier method returns a unique identifier for the object, based on its name. The class is designed to prevent copying, with private copy constructor and assignment operator, ensuring unique instances of objects.

('group___body_form_flags', 'BodyFormFla.name: BodyFormConstants
('group___body_form_flags', 'BodyFormFla.brief: Constants representing various body forms and attributes for mobs.
('group___body_form_flags', 'BodyFormFla.details: These constants define the physical and magical properties of mob bodies in the game.

('class_garbage', 'Garbage').name: Garbage
('class_garbage', 'Garbage').brief: Encapsulates the state of an object being considered as garbage or not.
('class_garbage', 'Garbage').details: The Garbage class provides a simple interface to manage and query the state of an object being considered as garbage. It includes methods to mark the object as garbage or not, and to check its current state. By default, an instance of Garbage is initialized as garbage. The not_garbage() method sets the state to non-garbage, while make_garbage() sets it to garbage. The is_garbage() method allows querying the current state, returning true if the object is considered garbage, and false otherwise.

('structconn_1_1_playing_state', 'conn::.name: conn::PlayingState
('structconn_1_1_playing_state', 'conn::.brief: Represents the state of a network connection when a character is actively playing.
('structconn_1_1_playing_state', 'conn::.details: The PlayingState struct is a subclass of the State class, representing the state of a network connection when a character is actively engaged in gameplay. It overrides the handleInput method to process input specific to the playing state, transitioning to a new state as necessary. The handleInput method takes a Descriptor pointer, which provides context for the network connection, and a constant reference to a String object, representing the input to be processed. This struct is crucial for managing the dynamic behavior of a character during gameplay, ensuring that inputs are handled appropriately and state transitions occur seamlessly.

('class_edit', 'Edit').name: Edit
('class_edit', 'Edit').brief: Encapsulates the state and operations for editing text.
('class_edit', 'Edit').details: The Edit class manages the state and operations related to editing text. It includes properties such as the type of edit, the line being edited, the number of lines affected, and whether an undo operation is possible. The class also maintains the current edit string and a backup of it. The edit ID uniquely identifies each edit operation. The class is designed to be non-copyable to prevent unintended copying of edit states.

('structconn_1_1_confirm_new_pass_state'.name: conn::ConfirmNewPassState
('structconn_1_1_confirm_new_pass_state'.brief: Handles the confirmation of a new password in a network connection state.
('structconn_1_1_confirm_new_pass_state'.details: The ConfirmNewPassState struct is a specialized state within a network connection's state machine, responsible for managing the confirmation process of a new password. It inherits from the State class and overrides the handleInput method to process input related to password confirmation. This struct ensures that the input provided matches the expected new password, facilitating a transition to the next appropriate state based on the input validation.

('group___equipment_wear_locations', 'Eq.name: EquipmentWearLocations
('group___equipment_wear_locations', 'Eq.brief: Constants representing locations where equipment can be worn.
('group___equipment_wear_locations', 'Eq.details: These constants define the possible locations on a character where items can be equipped.

('class_garbage_collecting_list_1_1itera.name: GarbageCollectingList::iterator
('class_garbage_collecting_list_1_1itera.brief: An iterator for traversing a GarbageCollectingList while skipping garbage elements.
('class_garbage_collecting_list_1_1itera.details: The iterator class provides functionality to traverse a GarbageCollectingList, automatically skipping over elements marked as garbage. It is constructed with an iterator pointing to an element in a standard list and a reference to the GarbageCollectingList it will be associated with. The iterator supports standard operations such as assignment, increment, dereferencing, and comparison. The increment operation ensures that the iterator skips over any elements that are considered garbage, as determined by the is_garbage() method of the elements.

('classworldmap_1_1_map_color', 'worldma.name: worldmap::MapColor
('classworldmap_1_1_map_color', 'worldma.brief: Encapsulates an RGB color and provides comparison operations.
('classworldmap_1_1_map_color', 'worldma.details: The MapColor class represents a color using red, green, and blue components, each as an unsigned integer. It provides constructors for initializing colors, including a copy constructor and an assignment operator. The class supports equality and inequality comparisons, as well as a less-than comparison based on a precomputed integer value derived from the RGB components. The precompute function calculates this integer value, which is used for efficient comparison operations. The class also defines a static constant for an uncolored state.

('class_war', 'War').name: War
('class_war', 'War').brief: Encapsulates the state and events of a war, including opponents and event history.
('class_war', 'War').details: The War class manages the details of a war, including the opponents involved and the events that occur during the war. It contains nested classes, Opponent and Event, which represent the participants and the chronological events of the war, respectively. Each Opponent has attributes such as name, clan name, and scores, and is linked in a list structure to allow for multiple opponents. The Event class captures details of each event, including type, associated strings, a number, and a timestamp, also using a linked list structure to maintain the sequence of events. The War class itself maintains arrays of challenger and defender opponents, a linked list of events, and pointers to previous and next War instances, allowing for a chain of wars. The class ensures proper management of resources by disallowing copy construction and assignment.

('group___target_types', 'TargetTypes').name: TargetTypes
('group___target_types', 'TargetTypes').brief: Constants representing types of targets for spells and abilities.
('group___target_types', 'TargetTypes').details: These constants define the valid targets for spells, abilities, and other actions in the game.

('structcolor__type', 'color_type').name: color_type
('structcolor__type', 'color_type').brief: Represents a styled text element with a name, code, color, and boldness.
('structcolor__type', 'color_type').details: This struct encapsulates a styled text element, providing fields for a name, a code, a color, and a bold attribute. The 'name' and 'code' fields are of type String, which extends std::string with additional functionality, including case-insensitive comparisons. The 'color' field is an integer representing the color code, and the 'bold' field is an integer indicating whether the text should be bold (non-zero value) or not (zero value). This struct can be used to manage and display text elements with specific styles in a user interface or text processing application.

('structconn_1_1_read_new_m_o_t_d_state'.name: conn::ReadNewMOTDState
('structconn_1_1_read_new_m_o_t_d_state'.brief: Represents the state for handling user input related to reading the Message of the Day (MOTD).
('structconn_1_1_read_new_m_o_t_d_state'.details: ReadNewMOTDState is a derived struct from the base State class, specifically designed to manage the input handling process when a user is reading or interacting with the Message of the Day (MOTD). It overrides the handleInput method to process user input within this context, facilitating transitions to other states or executing relevant actions based on the input received during the MOTD reading phase.

('group___help_flags', 'HelpFlags').name: help_flags
('group___help_flags', 'HelpFlags').details: here, and not in the table, is where order is important. helps are sorted by precedence of their type, so imm helps will follow mortal helps, etc. if any changes to the order need to be made, make the change, then wipe the table and reload fresh, because loadhelps() will not wipe the table properly if numbers don't match up. same goes for if any categories are removed

('structattack__type', 'attack_type').name: attack_type
('structattack__type', 'attack_type').brief: Represents an entity with a name, a descriptive noun, and a damage class.
('structattack__type', 'attack_type').details: This struct encapsulates the attributes of an entity, including its name, a descriptive noun for messaging purposes, and an integer representing its damage class. The name and noun are stored as String objects, which extend the functionality of std::string by providing additional features such as case-insensitive comparisons. The damage class is an integer value that can be used to quantify or categorize the potential impact or severity associated with the entity.

('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'worldmap::.name: worldmap::Region
('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'worldmap::.brief: Represents a geographical region with spatial alignment, color mapping, and image data for use in a world map or game environment.
('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'worldmap::.details: The Region class encapsulates a specific area within a map, managing its visual representation via an image, spatial alignment parameters, and color-to-vnum mappings. It provides methods to convert between color and vnum identifiers, retrieve pixel colors and vnums at specific coordinates, and create in-game rooms based on the region's data. The class maintains sets of allowed mobile and room reset vnums, facilitating controlled spawning and reset behaviors. It is constructed with an Area reference and a file pointer for loading region data, and it ensures proper resource management through its destructor. The class also includes private copy constructor and assignment operator to prevent unintended copying.

('structaffect_1_1fn__data__container__t.name: affect::fn_data_container_type
('structaffect_1_1fn__data__container__t.brief: A container for passing an affect enum value as a data parameter by pointer.
('structaffect_1_1fn__data__container__t.details: The fn_data_container_type struct is designed to encapsulate an affect::type enumeration value, allowing it to be passed efficiently as a data parameter by pointer. This is particularly useful in scenarios where affect types need to be manipulated or accessed within functions that require data to be passed by reference, ensuring minimal overhead and maintaining type safety.

('group___size_constants', 'SizeConstant.name: SizeConstants
('group___size_constants', 'SizeConstant.brief: Constants representing the sizes of characters and mobs.
('group___size_constants', 'SizeConstant.details: These constants define the possible sizes for characters and mobs in the game.

('structaffect_1_1fn__params', 'affect::.name: affect::fn_params
('structaffect_1_1fn__params', 'affect::.brief: Encapsulates parameters for a function affecting an owner object.
('structaffect_1_1fn__params', 'affect::.details: The affect_fn_params struct is used to pass parameters to functions that modify or affect an owner object. It contains a pointer to the owner object, a function pointer to a modifier function that operates on the owner, and a generic data pointer for additional context or information needed by the modifier function. The struct is designed to be flexible, allowing different types of owner objects and modifier functions to be used.

('structconn_1_1_get_name_state', 'conn:.name: conn::GetNameState
('structconn_1_1_get_name_state', 'conn:.brief: Represents the state responsible for handling name input during character creation or naming processes.
('structconn_1_1_get_name_state', 'conn:.details: The GetNameState struct inherits from the State class and encapsulates the behavior for processing user input when a player is providing or confirming a character name. Its handleInput method processes the input string, performs validation checks such as name acceptability, existence, or denial, and transitions to the next appropriate state based on the input and validation results. This state is integral to the character creation workflow, ensuring that the chosen name adheres to game rules and is available for use.

('group___object_vnums', 'ObjectVnums').name: ObjectVirtualNumbers
('group___object_vnums', 'ObjectVnums').brief: Constants representing well-known object virtual numbers.
('group___object_vnums', 'ObjectVnums').details: These constants define specific object types and their virtual numbers used in the game.

('structrevoke__type', 'revoke_type').name: revoke_type
('structrevoke__type', 'revoke_type').brief: Encapsulates a named flag with an associated message.
('structrevoke__type', 'revoke_type').details: This struct represents a named flag using a combination of a name, a bit flag from the Flags::Bit enumeration, and an associated message. The name is stored as a String, allowing for versatile string operations and case-insensitive comparisons. The bit field holds the specific flag or combination of flags from the Flags::Bit enumeration. The message provides additional context or description related to the flag, also stored as a String.

('class_string', 'String').name: String
('class_string', 'String').brief: A versatile string class that extends std::string with additional functionality and case-insensitive comparisons.
('class_string', 'String').details: The String class inherits from std::string and Actable, providing a rich set of string manipulation methods beyond the standard library. It supports case-insensitive comparisons by default, making it suitable for applications where case sensitivity is not required. The class includes methods for finding substrings, checking prefixes, infixes, and suffixes, and determining if the string contains specific words. It also offers various transformation methods such as capitalization, case conversion, and whitespace trimming. Additionally, the class provides enhanced splitting, insertion, and replacement operations, as well as a method to center the string. The String class ensures robustness by handling null pointers gracefully and ignoring out-of-bounds errors in certain operations. As part of the Actable interface, it can return a unique identifier for the object, which is the string itself.

('structwis__app__type', 'wis_app_type').name: wis_app_type
('structwis__app__type', 'wis_app_type').details: This struct holds an integer value representing a practice. It can be used to store and manipulate practice-related data within the application.

('group___revoked_channel_flags', 'Revok.name: RevokedChannelFlags
('group___revoked_channel_flags', 'Revok.brief: Constants representing restrictions on communication channels.
('group___revoked_channel_flags', 'Revok.details: These flags define the channels that players or mobs are restricted from using.

('structeq__meta__t', 'eq_meta_t').name: eq_meta_t
('structeq__meta__t', 'eq_meta_t').brief: Encapsulates metadata for equipment rolls, including chance and roll details.
('structeq__meta__t', 'eq_meta_t').details: The eq_meta_t struct is used to store metadata related to equipment rolls in a game or simulation. It contains an integer 'chance' which represents the probability or likelihood of a particular event or outcome occurring. Additionally, it holds a reference to a constant vector of eq_roll_t objects, 'eq_rolls', which provides detailed information about each equipment roll, including its type and virtual number. This struct is useful for managing and evaluating the potential outcomes of equipment-related events.

('structdex__app__type', 'dex_app_type').name: dex_app_type
('structdex__app__type', 'dex_app_type').brief: Represents a defensive attribute for an entity.
('structdex__app__type', 'dex_app_type').details: This struct encapsulates the defensive attribute of an entity, which can be used to determine how well the entity can withstand attacks. The 'defensive' member variable holds an integer value representing the strength of the entity's defense.

('classworldmap_1_1_worldmap', 'worldmap.name: worldmap::Worldmap
('classworldmap_1_1_worldmap', 'worldmap.brief: Encapsulates a world map with sector retrieval based on coordinates.
('classworldmap_1_1_worldmap', 'worldmap.details: The Worldmap class provides functionality to represent a world map, allowing retrieval of sectors based on 2D coordinates. It manages the dimensions of the map and provides methods to access the height and width of the map. The class includes a private method to convert a MapColor to a Sector, which is used internally to determine the sector corresponding to a given coordinate. The Worldmap class ensures encapsulation by disallowing copy construction and assignment, and it manages its own memory for storing map rows. The class is designed to be extended, as indicated by its virtual destructor.

('structconn_1_1_get_old_pass_state', 'c.name: conn::GetOldPassState
('structconn_1_1_get_old_pass_state', 'c.brief: Represents a state in the input handling state machine for retrieving an old password during authentication.
('structconn_1_1_get_old_pass_state', 'c.details: GetOldPassState is a derived struct from State that encapsulates the behavior for handling user input when requesting the old password during login or password change procedures. Its handleInput method processes the input received from a Descriptor, typically validating or storing the password, and transitions to the next appropriate state based on the input. This struct is part of a state-driven system managing user interactions during authentication processes in the network connection context.

('class_descriptor', 'Descriptor').name: Descriptor
('class_descriptor', 'Descriptor').brief: Encapsulates the state and behavior of a network connection descriptor.
('class_descriptor', 'Descriptor').details: The Descriptor class represents a network connection channel, managing the state and data associated with a connection. It includes information about the connected character, the host address, and input/output buffers. The class supports operations to check if the connection is currently active and maintains pointers to other descriptors for linked operations. It is designed to handle input commands and manage output data, with additional fields for connection state and timing. The class is non-copyable to prevent unintended duplication of connection state.

('struct_cle_t__type', 'CleT_type').name: CleT_type
('struct_cle_t__type', 'CleT_type').details: This struct holds two String objects, 'male' and 'female', which are used to store and manage string representations for male and female genders, respectively. It extends the functionality of std::string by providing additional features such as case-insensitive comparisons, making it versatile for various applications where gender-specific strings are needed.

('class_room_prototype', 'RoomPrototype'.name: RoomPrototype
('class_room_prototype', 'RoomPrototype'.brief: Encapsulates the prototype for a room within a game area.
('class_room_prototype', 'RoomPrototype'.details: The RoomPrototype class defines the blueprint for creating rooms in a game area. It holds essential information such as the room's virtual number (vnum), name, description, and sector type. The class also manages room-specific properties like healing and mana regeneration rates, ownership, and guild restrictions. It includes pointers to additional descriptive data and exit prototypes for constructing room connections. The RoomPrototype is associated with a specific area and can be linked to a clan. The class ensures that each room created from this prototype is counted, and it provides an equality operator to compare room prototypes based on their vnum. The class is designed to prevent copying and assignment to maintain the integrity of the room prototype data.

('classworldmap_1_1_quadtree', 'worldmap.name: worldmap::Quadtree
('classworldmap_1_1_quadtree', 'worldmap.brief: A Quadtree data structure for spatial partitioning and efficient data storage and retrieval.
('classworldmap_1_1_quadtree', 'worldmap.details: The Quadtree class is a spatial data structure that efficiently stores and retrieves data associated with 2D coordinates. It divides space into four quadrants, recursively, to manage data points. Each Quadtree node can store a single data point if its capacity is one, or it can have up to four children representing the northwest, northeast, southwest, and southeast quadrants. The class provides methods to insert data (put), retrieve data (get), and remove data based on coordinates. The Quadtree is initialized with a specified capacity, which determines the granularity of the spatial partitioning. The class also includes private methods to calculate the appropriate child index for a given coordinate and to check if a node is in use. The Quadtree does not own the data it stores, meaning it does not manage the memory of the data pointers.

('structguild__type', 'guild_type').name: guild_type
('structguild__type', 'guild_type').brief: Represents a character class with attributes and skills.
('structguild__type', 'guild_type').details: This struct defines a character class in a game, encapsulating various attributes and skills associated with the class. It includes the full name of the class and a three-letter abbreviation for quick reference. The struct specifies the prime attribute that is most important for the class, the initial weapon proficiency, and the maximum skill level attainable. It also defines the Thac0 (To Hit Armor Class 0) values for levels 0 and 32, which are used to calculate the likelihood of hitting a target. Additionally, the struct lists the base and default skill groups that the class gains. Health points (hp), mana, and stamina gains per level are also defined, with minimum and maximum values to indicate the range of possible increases as the character levels up.

('group___player_flags', 'PlayerFlags').name: PlayerActionFlags
('group___player_flags', 'PlayerFlags').brief: Constants representing various act flags for players.
('group___player_flags', 'PlayerFlags').details:
Constants representing various action flags for players.
These flags define automatic behaviors, personal preferences, penalties, and other attributes for players in the game.
These flags define automatic behaviors, personal preferences, penalties, and other attributes for players in the game.

('structs__spell__info', 's_spell_info').name: s_spell_info
('structs__spell__info', 's_spell_info').brief: Encapsulates information about a spell, including its type and level.
('structs__spell__info', 's_spell_info').details: The s_spell_info struct is used to store information about a spell or skill, specifically its type and the level at which it is available to the current character. The 'type' field is an enumeration that specifies the category or nature of the spell or skill, while the 'level' field indicates the level requirement or proficiency of the character with respect to this spell or skill.

('group___color_constants', 'ColorConsta.name: ColorConstants
('group___color_constants', 'ColorConsta.brief: Constants representing color codes and attributes for text formatting.
('group___color_constants', 'ColorConsta.details:
The first number (bold = 0,1) gives you dim or BRIGHT colors. The second number determines the colors as follows:
30 = grey
31 = red
32 = green
33 = yellow
34 = blue
35 = purple
36 = cyan
37 = white
Special notes:
On a Mac, the colors may be screwy.
0,30 gives you black.
0,34 seems to be REALLY dark so it doesn't show up too well.

('class_disabled', 'Disabled').name: Disabled
('class_disabled', 'Disabled').brief: Encapsulates a disabled command entry with metadata.
('class_disabled', 'Disabled').details: The Disabled class represents a node in a linked list of disabled commands. Each instance contains a pointer to the next node, a pointer to a command entry of type cmd_type, and metadata about the command's disabler and the reason for its disabling. The class provides a default constructor and a virtual destructor, ensuring proper cleanup in derived classes. Copy construction and assignment are disabled to prevent unintended copying of nodes.

('structconn_1_1_state', 'conn::State').name: conn::State
('structconn_1_1_state', 'conn::State').brief: A collection of static instances representing various connection states and a virtual method for handling input in a network connection context.
('structconn_1_1_state', 'conn::State').details: This struct serves as a centralized repository of static state objects corresponding to different stages and actions within a network connection lifecycle, such as connection termination, character creation, login procedures, and gameplay states. It also includes a virtual method handleInput that processes input from a Descriptor based on the current state, enabling polymorphic behavior for handling user interactions and connection transitions in the application.

('group___mob_act_flags', 'MobActFlags').name: MobActFlags
('group___mob_act_flags', 'MobActFlags').brief: Constants representing behavior flags for mobs.
('group___mob_act_flags', 'MobActFlags').details: These flags define various behaviors and attributes for mobs in the game.

('group___damage_type_vulnerability_flag.name: DamageTypeVulnerabilities
('group___damage_type_vulnerability_flag.brief: Constants representing vulnerabilities to various types of damage.
('group___damage_type_vulnerability_flag.details: These constants define the types of damage that mobs are vulnerable to in the game.

('group___censor_flags', 'CensorFlags').name: CensorFlags
('group___censor_flags', 'CensorFlags').brief: Constants representing censorship flags for communication channels and spam control.
('group___censor_flags', 'CensorFlags').details: These flags define the censorship settings for channels and spam filtering in the game.

('structitem__type', 'item_type').name: item_type
('structitem__type', 'item_type').brief: Represents an entity with a type identifier and a name.
('structitem__type', 'item_type').details: This struct encapsulates an entity characterized by an integer type and a string name. The 'type' field is used to specify the category or classification of the entity, while the 'name' field holds the name or identifier of the entity as a string. This struct can be used in various contexts where entities need to be categorized and named, such as in object management systems or data processing applications.

('structconn_1_1_get_new_pass_state', 'c.name: conn::GetNewPassState
('structconn_1_1_get_new_pass_state', 'c.brief: Represents the state for handling new password input in a network connection.
('structconn_1_1_get_new_pass_state', 'c.details: The GetNewPassState struct is a specialized state within a network connection's state machine, responsible for processing input related to setting or changing a password. It inherits from the State class and overrides the handleInput method to manage the transition based on the input received. This state is typically used when a user is prompted to enter a new password, ensuring that the input is processed correctly and the connection transitions to the appropriate next state.

('structnew__pose__struct', 'new_pose_st.name: new_pose_struct
('structnew__pose__struct', 'new_pose_st.brief: Represents messages related to a pose action.
('structnew__pose__struct', 'new_pose_st.details: The new_pose_struct encapsulates messages that are used when a pose action is performed. It contains two string members: self_msg, which is the message intended for the individual performing the pose, and room_msg, which is the message broadcasted to others in the room.

('group___color_escape_constants', 'Colo.name: ColorEscapeConstants
('group___color_escape_constants', 'Colo.brief: Constants representing ANSI escape codes for text formatting.
('group___color_escape_constants', 'Colo.details: Colour definitions by Lope of Loping Through The MUD. These constants are used to define ANSI escape sequences for various text formatting options, such as colors and styles.

('group___rank_types', 'RankTypes').name: GeneralRanks
('group___rank_types', 'RankTypes').brief: Constants representing general ranks for immortals and players.
('group___rank_types', 'RankTypes').details: These constants define the hierarchy of ranks used for comparisons and command types in the game.

('structfield__type', 'field_type').name: field_type
('structfield__type', 'field_type').brief: Encapsulates configuration options and visibility settings for entities.
('structfield__type', 'field_type').details: This struct represents a configuration entity with a name and a reference to a table of flag_type options. It includes integer fields to manage candidate status and visibility modifiers for both mobile and player entities. The 'cand' field indicates the candidate status, while 'see_mob' and 'see_plr' determine the visibility of mobile and player entities, respectively. The 'mod_mob' and 'mod_plr' fields are used to modify the visibility settings for mobile and player entities.

('group___item_types', 'ItemTypes').name: ItemTypes
('group___item_types', 'ItemTypes').brief: Constants representing various item types in the game.
('group___item_types', 'ItemTypes').details: These constants define the types of items that can exist in the game, such as weapons, armor, and consumables.

('group___mob_vnums', 'MobVnums').name: MobVirtualNumbers
('group___mob_vnums', 'MobVnums').brief: Constants representing well-known mob virtual numbers and group identifiers.
('group___mob_vnums', 'MobVnums').details: These constants are used to define specific mob types and groups in the game.

('classevent_1_1_handler', 'event::Handl.name: event::Handler
('classevent_1_1_handler', 'event::Handl.brief: Abstract base class for handling event notifications.
('classevent_1_1_handler', 'event::Handl.details: The Handler class serves as an abstract base class for handling event notifications. It declares a pure virtual function 'notify' that must be implemented by derived classes to handle specific event types and associated arguments. The class provides a protected default constructor to allow instantiation by derived classes, while preventing direct instantiation. Copy construction and assignment are disabled to ensure unique handler instances. The destructor is virtual to ensure proper cleanup of derived class objects.

('structconn_1_1_break_connect_state', '.name: conn::BreakConnectState
('structconn_1_1_break_connect_state', '.brief: Represents a state where a network connection is being broken or terminated.
('structconn_1_1_break_connect_state', '.details: The BreakConnectState struct is a specialized state within the connection management system, derived from the State class. It encapsulates the behavior and logic for handling scenarios where a network connection is in the process of being broken or terminated. The handleInput method is overridden to process input specific to this state, potentially transitioning to a new state based on the input received. This struct plays a crucial role in managing the lifecycle of network connections, ensuring that appropriate actions are taken when a connection is disrupted.

('class_object_value', 'ObjectValue').name: ObjectValue
('class_object_value', 'ObjectValue').brief: Encapsulates an integer value with conversion and arithmetic capabilities.
('class_object_value', 'ObjectValue').details: The ObjectValue class provides a flexible representation of an integer value, allowing for conversion to and from Flags and Vnum types. It supports various arithmetic operations and assignment operators to manipulate the stored value. The class ensures that once constructed, the '_as_flags' state is preserved, preventing accidental reassignment. It includes both prefix and postfix increment and decrement operators, and offers comparison operators for equality checks. The class is designed to be used in scenarios where an integer value needs to be associated with additional state or behavior, such as in area loading and object creation from prototypes.

('classevent_1_1_dispatcher', 'event::Di.name: event::Dispatcher
('classevent_1_1_dispatcher', 'event::Di.brief: Manages event subscriptions and dispatches events to registered handlers.
('classevent_1_1_dispatcher', 'event::Di.details: The Dispatcher class is responsible for managing event subscriptions and notifying registered handlers when events occur. It allows handlers to subscribe to specific event types and ensures that when an event is fired, all subscribed handlers are notified with the appropriate arguments. The class maintains a collection of subscribers, where each subscriber is associated with a specific event type. The Dispatcher provides methods to fire events, subscribe handlers to events, and unsubscribe handlers from events. It prevents copying and assignment to ensure the integrity of the subscription list.

('class_area', 'Area').name: Area
('class_area', 'Area').brief: Represents a game area, managing its components, prototypes, rooms, and state information.
('class_area', 'Area').details: This class encapsulates the data and behaviors associated with a game area, including loading area data from files, managing prototypes for objects, mobiles, and rooms, handling resets, and tracking player and immortal counts. It provides methods to access specific prototypes and rooms, reset the area, and update its state. Additionally, it maintains descriptive metadata such as author, title, keywords, and version, as well as spatial and regional information. The class interacts closely with the world and other game entities to facilitate dynamic game environment management.

('group___visibility_types', 'Visibility.name: VisibilityConstants
('group___visibility_types', 'Visibility.brief: Constants defining types of visibility for characters.
('group___visibility_types', 'Visibility.details:
There are 2 types of invisibility:
All-encompassing (wizi, superwiz, lurk), which are outside gameplay.
Gameplay invisibility (invis, hide, sneak, midnight), which are for in-game use only.
These constants are used in get_char functions to return victims the character can see in either case.  Montrey

('class_weather', 'Weather').name: Weather
('class_weather', 'Weather').brief: Encapsulates weather conditions and their updates based on game time.
('class_weather', 'Weather').details: The Weather class manages the current weather conditions within the game world, including atmospheric pressure and sky state. It utilizes the GameTime object to determine and update these conditions over time. The class provides a method to describe the current weather and maintains an enumeration of possible sky states, such as Cloudless, Cloudy, Raining, and Lightning. Atmospheric pressure is represented by the mmhg attribute, and change indicates the variation in pressure. The class is designed to be non-copyable to ensure unique weather instances, and it relies on the singleton instance of GameTime for time-related operations.

('group___ban_flags', 'BanFlags').name: BanFlags
('group___ban_flags', 'BanFlags').brief: Constants representing flags for banning players or connections.
('group___ban_flags', 'BanFlags').details: These flags are used to specify the type of ban applied to players or connections.

('structconn_1_1_confirm_new_name_state'.name: conn::ConfirmNewNameState
('structconn_1_1_confirm_new_name_state'.brief: Represents the state for confirming a new name in a network connection.
('structconn_1_1_confirm_new_name_state'.details: The ConfirmNewNameState struct is a specialized state within a network connection's state machine, responsible for handling the confirmation of a new name input by the user. It inherits from the State class and overrides the handleInput method to process input related to name confirmation. This struct ensures that the input is validated and transitions the connection to the appropriate next state based on the user's response.

('group___color_slot_types', 'ColorSlotT.name: ColorSlotConstants
('group___color_slot_types', 'ColorSlotT.brief: Color slot constants for player customizable stuff  Elrac (Expanded by Xenith)

('class_reset', 'Reset').name: Reset
('class_reset', 'Reset').brief: Encapsulates the definition and initialization of an area reset in a game world.
('class_reset', 'Reset').details: The Reset class is responsible for defining and initializing area resets within a game world. It reads reset data from a file stream during construction, allowing the game to set up initial conditions for areas. The class stores command and argument values that dictate the specific reset actions to be performed. The constructor initializes these values by reading from a provided FILE pointer, while the destructor ensures proper cleanup. The class is non-copyable and non-assignable to prevent unintended duplication of reset configurations.

('group___armor_class_types', 'ArmorClas.name: ArmorClassTypes
('group___armor_class_types', 'ArmorClas.brief: Constants representing types of armor class.
('group___armor_class_types', 'ArmorClas.details: These constants define the types of armor class used for damage mitigation in the game.

('class_pooled', 'Pooled').name: Pooled
('class_pooled', 'Pooled').brief: A template class for managing a pool of reusable objects of type T.
('class_pooled', 'Pooled').details: The Pooled class implements a self-contained pool of free objects of type T to optimize resource management by minimizing the creation and destruction of frequently used objects. To use this class, subclass it with a specific type, ensuring that Pooled is the first inherited class (e.g., SomeClass : public Pooled<SomeClass>). It is crucial not to subclass a class that already inherits from Pooled, as each class must maintain its own object pool to prevent incorrect object size allocation. The class provides static methods to manage the pool, including allocating new objects, deleting objects back to the pool, and destroying the entire pool when necessary. The pool_allocated method returns a reference to a static counter of allocated resources, while the free_list method returns a reference to a static vector containing pointers to free objects of type T. This design is based on work by Thomas George, 2003, and can be found at: https://www.codeproject.com/Articles/3968/Object-Pooling-for-Generic-C-classes.

('struct_war_t__type', 'WarT_type').name: WarT_type
('struct_war_t__type', 'WarT_type').details: This struct holds two String objects, 'male' and 'female', which are used to store gender-specific string values. The String class extends std::string, providing additional functionality and case-insensitive comparisons, allowing for versatile handling of gender-related text data.

('structpc__race__type', 'pc_race_type').name: pc_race_type
('structpc__race__type', 'pc_race_type').brief: Encapsulates the attributes and characteristics of a race in the game.
('structpc__race__type', 'pc_race_type').details: This struct defines the properties of a race, including its name, display name for 'who' listings, base age, and point cost. It also includes experience multipliers for different guilds, starting and maximum stats, and size attributes. Additionally, it specifies the remort level required for the race and lists any bonus skills associated with it. The 'name' and 'who_name' fields are of type String, which allows for case-insensitive comparisons and additional string functionalities.

('structhelpfile__table__type', 'helpfil.name: helpfile_table_type
