('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_bug(Character.brief: Logs a bug report from a character or displays recent bugs for immortals.
('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_bug(Character.details: The function allows a character to report a bug by providing a description. If the character is immortal and no description is provided, it displays the last 150 bug reports. If a description is provided, it appends the bug report to a designated bug file and confirms the logging to the character.

params.ch: A pointer to the Character object representing the character reporting the bug or viewing recent bugs.
params.argument: A String containing the bug description to be logged. If empty, recent bugs are displayed for immortal characters.

('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_bug(Character.returns: This function does not return a value.

('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_typo(Characte.name: do_typo
('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_typo(Characte.brief: Handles the reporting of typos by a character.
('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_typo(Characte.details: The function allows a character to report a typo by providing a description. If no description is provided and the character is an immortal, it displays the last 150 lines of the 'typos' file. Otherwise, it prompts the character to provide a description of the typo. If a description is provided, it appends the description to the typo log file and notifies the character that the typo has been logged.

params.ch: A pointer to the Character object representing the character reporting the typo.
params.argument: A String containing the description of the typo to be reported. If empty, the function will prompt for a description or display recent typos if the character is an immortal.

('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_typo(Characte.returns: This function does not return a value.

('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_qui(Character.name: do_qui
('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_qui(Character.brief: Handles an incomplete quit command from a character.
('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_qui(Character.details: This function is called when a character attempts to quit the game using an incomplete command. It informs the character that they must spell out the full 'QUIT' command to successfully quit the game. The message is sent to the character's output buffer using the stc function, ensuring that the character receives feedback on their action.

params.ch: A pointer to the Character object representing the character attempting to quit.
params.argument: A String object containing the command argument provided by the character, which is not used in this function.

('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_qui(Character.returns: This function does not return a value.

('act__comm_8cc', 'bool showlost(Charact.name: showlost
('act__comm_8cc', 'bool showlost(Charact.brief: Recursively identifies and displays items in a character's inventory or locker that will be lost upon quitting.
('act__comm_8cc', 'bool showlost(Charact.details: This function traverses a linked list of objects, checking each for conditions that would cause the item to be lost if the character quits. It considers item level, type, and specific item values to determine if an item is at risk. When such items are found, it outputs their descriptions to the character. The function can operate on either the inventory or a locker, based on the 'locker' parameter, and it processes nested containers recursively. It returns a boolean indicating whether any items that will be lost were found during the traversal.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character object for whom the items are being checked.
params.obj: Pointer to the current Object in the linked list being examined.
params.found: Boolean flag indicating if any items that will be lost have been identified so far.
params.locker: Boolean indicating whether to check items in the locker (true) or in the inventory (false).

('act__comm_8cc', 'bool showlost(Charact.returns: A boolean value indicating whether any items that will be lost were found during the traversal.

('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_quit(Characte.name: do_quit
('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_quit(Characte.brief: Handles the process of a character quitting the game, including notifications, cleanup, and broadcasting.
('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_quit(Characte.details: This function manages a character's exit from the game environment. It performs checks to ensure the character is eligible to quit (not an NPC, not fighting, not involved in a duel, etc.), deducts locker fees if applicable, displays a quitting message, notifies other players of the departure, logs the event via wiznet, saves the character's state, resets terminal settings if needed, and finally removes the character from the game. It also handles disconnecting the character's network socket and ensures that any other descriptors associated with the same character ID are also properly closed and cleaned up.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character object representing the player attempting to quit.
params.argument: Additional string argument provided to the command, not used directly in this implementation.

('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_quit(Characte.returns: This function has no return value; it performs all operations as side effects, including character removal and network disconnection.

('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_fuckoff(Chara.name: do_fuckoff
('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_fuckoff(Chara.brief: Terminates a player character, broadcasts a notification, and cleans up associated connections and data.
('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_fuckoff(Chara.details: This function handles the forced removal of a player character from the game. It first checks if the character is a non-player character (NPC) and exits if so. For player characters, it logs and announces the termination, saves the character's data, and then extracts the character from the game world, closing their network connection. Additionally, it searches for and terminates any other active connections associated with the same character ID, ensuring all instances are properly cleaned up. This process is used primarily for administrative or disciplinary actions to forcibly disconnect and remove a player.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character object representing the player to be terminated.
params.argument: A String object containing any additional arguments or message (not used in this implementation).

('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_fuckoff(Chara.returns: Void; the function performs cleanup and does not return a value.

('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_backup(Charac.name: do_backup
('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_backup(Charac.brief: Performs a backup of a character's save file if the character is not an NPC.
('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_backup(Charac.details: This function checks if the provided character is a player character (not an NPC). If so, it sends a confirmation message to the character, invokes the backup_char_obj function to create a backup of the character's data, enforces a brief wait state, and then notifies the character that the backup is complete. It also updates the character's backup timestamp with the current playing time in seconds. The function ensures that NPCs are excluded from backup operations and provides user feedback during the process.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character object representing the player initiating the backup.
params.argument: Additional command arguments as a String; not used in this implementation.

('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_backup(Charac.returns: Void; the function performs side effects such as file backup and messaging without returning a value.

('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_save(Characte.name: do_save
('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_save(Characte.brief: Saves the character's data if they are a player character.
('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_save(Characte.details: This function checks whether the provided character is a non-player character (NPC) using is_npc(). If the character is a player character, it proceeds to save their data by calling save_char_obj(). It then sends a confirmation message to the character's output buffer indicating that the save has occurred, noting that the system has automatic saving. Finally, it sets the character's wait state to prevent immediate repeated actions, with the duration based on PULSE_VIOLENCE. If the character is an NPC, the function exits without performing any actions.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character object representing the character performing the save.
params.argument: Additional command arguments as a String; not used in this implementation.

('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_save(Characte.returns: Void; the function performs actions without returning a value.

('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_follow(Charac.name: do_follow
('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_follow(Charac.brief: Handles a character's attempt to follow another character in the game environment.
('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_follow(Charac.details: This function processes the follow command issued by a character, allowing them to follow another character present in the same room. It checks for various conditions such as whether the target is specified, present, and willing to accept followers (e.g., not under 'NOFOLLOW' restrictions). It also prevents characters from following themselves unless they are already following someone, and handles special states like charm effects. If valid, it establishes a master-follower relationship by calling add_follower, removing any existing follow relationships. The function provides appropriate feedback messages to the character based on the context and restrictions encountered.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character issuing the follow command.
params.argument: String containing the name of the character to follow.

('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_follow(Charac.returns: Void; the function performs side effects such as updating follow relationships and sending messages, with no return value.

('act__comm_8cc', 'void add_follower(Cha.name: add_follower
('act__comm_8cc', 'void add_follower(Cha.brief: Assigns a new follower to a character, establishing a master-follower relationship.
('act__comm_8cc', 'void add_follower(Cha.details:
Assigns a new follower (master) to a character if they are not already following someone.
This function sets the specified character 'ch' to follow the 'master' character. It first checks if 'ch' already has a master; if so, it logs a bug message and exits. Otherwise, it assigns 'master' as 'ch's master and clears 'ch's leader. If the master can see 'ch', it sends a message to the master indicating that 'ch' now follows them. Additionally, it notifies 'ch' that they are now following the master. The function does not return a value.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character object to be assigned as a follower.
params.master: Pointer to the Character object who will become the new master.

('act__comm_8cc', 'void add_follower(Cha.returns: This function has no return value; it performs side effects such as updating follower relationships and sending messages.

('act__comm_8cc', 'void stop_follower(Ch.name: stop_follower
('act__comm_8cc', 'void stop_follower(Ch.brief: Stops a character from following their current master and clears related affect states.
('act__comm_8cc', 'void stop_follower(Ch.details: This function terminates the following relationship of the specified character by removing charm affects, updating master and pet references, and notifying involved characters if visible. It first checks if the character has a master; if not, it logs a bug and exits. If the character is affected by charm, that affect is removed. If the master can see the character, appropriate messages are displayed to both parties. If the character is a pet of the master, the pet reference is cleared. Finally, the character's master and leader pointers are set to null, effectively stopping the follow behavior.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character object who is stopping to follow their master.

('act__comm_8cc', 'void stop_follower(Ch.returns: This function has no return value; it performs side effects to update the follower relationship and dispatch messages.

('act__comm_8cc', 'void nuke_pets(Charac.name: nuke_pets
('act__comm_8cc', 'void nuke_pets(Charac.brief: Removes a character's pet and any associated followers from the game world.
('act__comm_8cc', 'void nuke_pets(Charac.details:
Removes a character's pet from the game world, stopping its following behavior and handling cleanup.
This function 'nuke_pets' targets the specified character's pet, if any, and performs cleanup by stopping its following behavior, displaying a fade-away message in the current room, and extracting the pet from the game world. It also clears the pet pointer in the character to prevent dangling references. The function ensures that the pet and any related follow relationships are properly terminated, effectively removing the pet entity from gameplay.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character whose pet is to be nuked.

('act__comm_8cc', 'void nuke_pets(Charac.returns: Void; the function performs side effects such as stopping follow behavior, messaging, and character extraction without returning a value.

('act__comm_8cc', 'void die_follower(Cha.name: die_follower
('act__comm_8cc', 'void die_follower(Cha.brief: Handles the process of a character dying and properly removing all follower and pet associations.
('act__comm_8cc', 'void die_follower(Cha.details:
Handles the process of a character dying and properly updating follower and master relationships.
This function manages the cleanup when a character dies by removing the character from any follower or pet relationships. It checks if the character has a master, and if so, clears the character from the master's pet or special entity pointers (such as skeleton, zombie, wraith, gargoyle). It then calls 'stop_follower' to cease following behavior and clears the character's leader pointer. Additionally, it iterates through all characters in the game world, stopping any followers that have the dying character as their master, and resets leader pointers for characters who follow the dying character, effectively removing all associations related to the character's death.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character object representing the character that is dying and whose follower relationships are to be cleaned up.

('act__comm_8cc', 'void die_follower(Cha.returns: Void; the function performs side effects such as updating relationships and stopping followers without returning a value.

('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_order(Charact.name: do_order
('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_order(Charact.brief: Executes an order command allowing a character to command their followers or familiar to perform actions.
('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_order(Charact.details: This function processes an 'order' command issued by a character, enabling them to give instructions to their followers, pets, or familiar. It handles various subcommands such as 'stay', 'follow', 'wimpy', and 'courage', affecting the follower's behavior. The function also supports ordering all followers in the room or remotely ordering a familiar if applicable. It performs checks for command validity, follower presence, and character states, then issues the command to the appropriate followers or familiar, updating their act flags or executing the specified action. It also manages command restrictions, such as preventing certain commands like 'delete' or 'mp' prefix commands, and ensures the character is not under charm effects that prevent giving orders.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character issuing the order command.
params.argument: String containing the full command and arguments, which is parsed to determine the target and action.

('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_order(Charact.returns: Void; the function performs command processing and follower actions without returning a value.

('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_pet(Character.name: do_pet
('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_pet(Character.brief: Allows a character to issue an order command specifically for their pet.
('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_pet(Character.details: This function enables a character to command their pet to perform an action or follow an order. It constructs a command string by extracting the pet's name and appending the provided argument, then invokes do_order() to execute the command. The function checks for the presence of a pet, validates the argument length, and formats the command appropriately before execution. It is intended to be used as a simplified way for players to give commands to their pets without using the standard 'order' command syntax.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character issuing the pet command.
params.argument: String containing the command or action to be performed by the pet.

('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_pet(Character.returns: Void; the function performs the command processing and does not return a value.

('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_group(Charact.name: do_group
('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_group(Charact.brief: Manages group membership for a character, allowing them to view their group or add/remove members.
('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_group(Charact.details: This function allows a character to either display their current group members or modify group membership by adding or removing a character. If called without arguments, it lists all characters in the caller's group, displaying their level, guild, health, mana, stamina, and experience needed for next level. If called with an argument, it attempts to find a character in the same room matching the argument, then adds or removes them from the caller's group based on current group status, following rules such as not allowing charmed characters to be grouped and ensuring the caller is not following someone else. It provides appropriate feedback messages for each action and enforces group integrity rules.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character object initiating the command.
params.argument: String containing either no arguments (to list group) or the name of a character to add/remove.

('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_group(Charact.returns: Void; the function performs side effects such as displaying messages and updating group relationships.

('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_split(Charact.name: do_split
('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_split(Charact.brief: Splits a specified amount of gold and silver coins among group members in the same room.
('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_split(Charact.details: This function allows a character to distribute a specified amount of gold and silver coins to all other group members present in the same room, excluding charm-affected characters. It first parses the input argument to determine the amounts of silver and gold to split. It performs validation checks for input validity, character's available funds, and group size. The total amount is divided evenly among group members, with any remainder distributed appropriately. The character's coin totals are adjusted accordingly, and each group member (excluding the initiator) receives their share with a notification message. The function ensures that no invalid or zero distributions occur and handles pluralization in messages.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character object initiating the split.
params.argument: String containing the amounts of silver and gold to split, e.g., '10 20'.

('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_split(Charact.returns: Void; the function performs side effects such as updating coin counts and sending messages to characters.

('act__comm_8cc', 'bool is_same_group(Ch.name: is_same_group
('act__comm_8cc', 'bool is_same_group(Ch.brief: Determines if two characters belong to the same group.
('act__comm_8cc', 'bool is_same_group(Ch.details: This function checks if two Character objects, 'ach' and 'bch', are in the same group by comparing their leaders. If either character is null, the function returns false. If a character has a leader, the function considers the leader as the representative of the group. The function returns true if both characters have the same leader or are the same character, ensuring the equivalence relation properties.

params.ach: A pointer to the first Character object to compare.
params.bch: A pointer to the second Character object to compare.

('act__comm_8cc', 'bool is_same_group(Ch.returns: Returns true if both characters are in the same group, otherwise returns false.

('act__comm_8cc', 'void align(Character.name: align
('act__comm_8cc', 'void align(Character.brief: Changes a character's alignment if conditions are met.
('act__comm_8cc', 'void align(Character.details: The align function attempts to change the alignment of a character to a new specified value. It first checks if the character has enough stamina to perform the alignment change. If stamina is sufficient, it then determines success based on the character's skill level and a random percentile check. On success, the character's alignment is updated, and a success message is sent to the character. On failure, a failure message is sent. In both cases, the function checks and potentially improves the character's alignment skill. The function also imposes a wait state on the character after attempting the alignment change.

params.ch: A pointer to the Character object whose alignment is being changed.
params.new_align: An integer representing the new alignment value to be set for the character.
params.align_str: A pointer to a character array containing the string representation of the new alignment.

('act__comm_8cc', 'void align(Character.returns: This function does not return a value.

('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_align(Charact.name: do_align
('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_align(Charact.brief: Handles the alignment change command for a character, allowing players to set their alignment to good, neutral, or evil.
('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_align(Charact.details: This function processes a command input to change a character's alignment. It first checks if the character is an NPC; if so, it informs that NPCs cannot change alignment. It then verifies if the character has the necessary skill to use the alignment change command via CAN_USE_RSKILL. If the command argument is empty, it displays syntax instructions. Otherwise, it extracts the first argument from the input and checks if it is a prefix of 'good', 'neutral', or 'evil', setting the character's alignment accordingly using the align() function. If the argument does not match any of these options, the function recursively calls itself with an empty argument to display the syntax message.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character object issuing the command, representing the player or entity attempting to change alignment.
params.argument: String containing the command argument specifying the desired alignment ('good', 'neutral', or 'evil').

('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_align(Charact.returns: This function does not return a value; it performs side effects such as sending messages to the character and updating their alignment.

('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_outfit(Charac.name: do_outfit
('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_outfit(Charac.brief: Equips a character with basic gear if they are below a certain level.
('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_outfit(Charac.details: The function 'do_outfit' equips a character with basic equipment if their level is 5 or below and they are not a non-player character (NPC). It checks if the character already has certain items equipped, such as a light source, body armor, a weapon, and a shield. If any of these items are missing, it creates them from predefined prototypes and equips them to the character. The function also logs errors if object creation fails and sends a message to the character indicating they have been equipped.

params.ch: A pointer to the Character object that is to be equipped.
params.argument: A String object representing additional arguments passed to the function, though it is not used in this implementation.

('act__comm_8cc', 'void do_outfit(Charac.returns: This function does not return a value.

('structconn_1_1_get_weapon_state', 'Sta.name: handleInput
('structconn_1_1_get_weapon_state', 'Sta.brief: Handles input to select a weapon and transitions to the next state.
('structconn_1_1_get_weapon_state', 'Sta.details: This function processes the input provided by the user to select a weapon. It checks if the input corresponds to a valid weapon and if the character has learned the associated skill. If the weapon is valid and the skill is learned, it sets the learned value of the skill to 40, displays an automated message of the day, and transitions to the 'readMOTD' state. If the input is invalid, it prompts the user to make another selection and remains in the current state.

params.d: A pointer to a Descriptor object that provides context for the character making the weapon selection.
params.argument: A constant reference to a String object representing the weapon name input by the user.

('structconn_1_1_get_weapon_state', 'Sta.returns: A pointer to a State object representing the next state, which is 'readMOTD' if a valid weapon is selected, or the current state if the selection is invalid.

('argument_8cc', 'int number_argument(co.name: number_argument
('argument_8cc', 'int number_argument(co.brief: Extracts a numeric prefix from a string and returns the number and the remaining string.
('argument_8cc', 'int number_argument(co.details:
Extracts a numeric value from a string and modifies the argument.
This function processes a string formatted as 'number.suffix', extracting the numeric prefix and returning it as an integer. The suffix is copied into a separate buffer. If the string does not contain a valid numeric prefix followed by a dot, the entire string is copied into the buffer, and the function returns 1. The function assumes that the input string is well-formed and that the buffer provided for the suffix is sufficiently large.

params.argument: A pointer to the input C-style string to be analyzed for a leading numeric value.
params.arg: A pointer to a character array where the non-numeric portion of 'argument' will be stored if a numeric value is found.

('argument_8cc', 'int number_argument(co.returns: The integer value extracted from the beginning of 'argument' if a numeric value followed by a dot is found; otherwise, returns 1.

('argument_8cc', 'Flags::Bit entity_argu.name: entity_argument
('argument_8cc', 'Flags::Bit entity_argu.brief: Parses an entity type from a string argument and modifies the argument.
('argument_8cc', 'Flags::Bit entity_argu.details:
Determines the entity type from a string argument and processes the argument.
This function scans a given string argument to identify and extract an entity type prefix ('p.', 'm.', 'o.', 'r.') or a variant prefix ('m#', 'o#', 'r#') from the beginning or after a numeric prefix. It modifies the provided character array 'arg' to contain the remaining part of the argument after the entity type or variant prefix is removed. The function is designed to handle cases where the argument starts with a number followed by a dot, and it recombines the number with the remaining argument if necessary. The function should not be used with 'argument' and 'arg' pointing to the same memory location, as this can cause undefined behavior.

params.argument: The input string expected to contain a numeric prefix followed by a character indicating the entity type.
params.arg: A buffer where the suffix of the input string will be copied after processing the numeric prefix and entity type.

('argument_8cc', 'Flags::Bit entity_argu.returns: A Flags::Bit value representing the identified entity type, or Flags::none if no valid entity type is found.

('argument_8cc', 'int mult_argument(cons.name: mult_argument
('argument_8cc', 'int mult_argument(cons.brief: Parses a string to separate a number and a substring after a '*' character.
('argument_8cc', 'int mult_argument(cons.details:
Parses a string to extract a multiplier and modifies the argument.
This function takes a string in the format of 'number*substring', extracts the number before the '*' as an integer, and copies the substring after the '*' into the provided buffer. If the '*' character is not found, the entire string is copied into the buffer, and the function returns 1.

params.argument: A pointer to a C-style string that may contain a numeric multiplier followed by an asterisk.
params.arg: A pointer to a character array where the substring after the asterisk will be stored if an asterisk is found.

('argument_8cc', 'int mult_argument(cons.returns: The numeric multiplier extracted from the input string if an asterisk is found, or 1 if no asterisk is present.

('argument_8cc', 'const char * one_argum.name: one_argument
('argument_8cc', 'const char * one_argum.brief: Extracts the first argument from a string, handling quotes, and returns the remainder.
('argument_8cc', 'const char * one_argum.details:
Extracts the first argument from a string.
This function processes a given input string to extract the first argument, which may be enclosed in single or double quotes. It skips leading whitespace, identifies the argument boundary based on spaces or matching quotes, and copies the argument into the provided buffer. The function then returns a pointer to the remainder of the string after the extracted argument, skipping any trailing whitespace.

params.argument: The input string from which the first argument is to be extracted.
params.arg_first: A buffer where the extracted argument will be stored.

('argument_8cc', 'const char * one_argum.returns: A pointer to the position in the input string where the next argument begins.

('argument_8cc', 'int number_argument(co.name: number_argument
('argument_8cc', 'int number_argument(co.brief: Extracts a numeric prefix from a String and returns the number and remaining suffix.
('argument_8cc', 'int number_argument(co.details:
Extracts a numeric prefix from a String and returns the number and the remaining string.
This function takes an input String expected to contain a numeric prefix followed by a dot and a suffix. It extracts the numeric prefix, converts it to an integer, and assigns the remaining suffix to the provided String reference. If no valid numeric prefix is found, it defaults to returning 1.

params.argument: The input String expected to contain a numeric prefix followed by a dot and a suffix.
params.arg: A String reference where the suffix of the input string will be copied after the numeric prefix and dot.

('argument_8cc', 'int number_argument(co.returns: The numeric value extracted from the prefix of the input string, or 1 if no valid numeric prefix is found.

('argument_8cc', 'Flags::Bit entity_argu.name: entity_argument
('argument_8cc', 'Flags::Bit entity_argu.brief: Parses an entity type from a string argument and modifies the argument.
('argument_8cc', 'Flags::Bit entity_argu.details: This function takes a String object representing an input argument and parses it to identify an entity type or variant. The identified entity type is returned as a Flags::Bit enumeration value. The function also modifies the provided output String reference to exclude the entity type or variant prefix, effectively storing the remaining part of the argument. It internally uses a character buffer to facilitate this operation.

params.argument: A constant reference to a String object containing the input string with the entity type and possibly a numeric prefix.
params.arg: A reference to a String object where the modified argument, excluding the entity type or variant prefix, will be stored.

('argument_8cc', 'Flags::Bit entity_argu.returns: A Flags::Bit enumeration value representing the identified entity type or variant type from the argument.

('argument_8cc', 'int mult_argument(cons.name: mult_argument
('argument_8cc', 'int mult_argument(cons.brief: Parses a String to extract a number and a substring after a '*' character.
('argument_8cc', 'int mult_argument(cons.details: This function takes a String object containing an input in the format 'number*substring'. It extracts the integer number before the '*' character and assigns the substring after '*' to the provided String reference 'arg'. If no '*' is present, the entire input is treated as a number and 'arg' is set to an empty string.

params.argument: A String object containing the input to be parsed.
params.arg: A reference to a String where the substring after '*' will be stored.

('argument_8cc', 'int mult_argument(cons.returns: The integer value of the number before the '*' character, or 1 if no '*' is present.

('argument_8cc', 'const char * one_argum.name: one_argument
('argument_8cc', 'const char * one_argum.brief: Extracts the first argument from a String and returns the remainder.
('argument_8cc', 'const char * one_argum.details: This function takes a String object containing a sequence of arguments and extracts the first argument, storing it in the provided String reference 'arg'. It handles quoted arguments correctly. The function returns a pointer to the remainder of the input string after the first argument has been extracted, with leading whitespace removed.

params.argument: The input String from which the first argument is to be extracted.
params.arg: A reference to a String where the extracted first argument will be stored.

('argument_8cc', 'const char * one_argum.returns: A pointer to the remainder of the input string after the first argument has been extracted, with leading whitespace removed.

('social_8cc', 'void insert_social(Socia.name: insert_social
('social_8cc', 'void insert_social(Socia.brief: Inserts a Social object into a sorted list alphabetically.
('social_8cc', 'void insert_social(Socia.details: This function inserts a Social object into a doubly linked list that is sorted alphabetically by the name of the Social objects. It traverses the list to find the correct position based on a case-insensitive comparison of names and inserts the new Social object at that position. If the list is empty, the function will append the Social object at the end of the list.

params.s: The Social object to be inserted into the list.

('social_8cc', 'void insert_social(Socia.returns: This function does not return a value.

('social_8cc', 'void remove_social(const.name: remove_social
('social_8cc', 'void remove_social(const.brief: Removes a social entry from the social table by name.
('social_8cc', 'void remove_social(const.details: This function searches for a social entry in the social table that matches the given name, ignoring case. If a matching entry is found, it is removed from the linked list and its memory is deallocated. The function assumes that the social table is implemented as a doubly linked list with head and tail sentinels.

params.name: The name of the social entry to be removed, compared in a case-insensitive manner.

('social_8cc', 'void remove_social(const.returns: This function does not return a value.

('social_8cc', 'int count_socials()').name: count_socials
('social_8cc', 'int count_socials()').brief: Counts the number of social objects in a linked list.
('social_8cc', 'int count_socials()').details:
Counts the number of social entries in a linked list.
This function iterates through a linked list of social objects, starting from the node following 'social_table_head' and ending at 'social_table_tail'. It counts each node in the list and returns the total count. The function assumes that 'social_table_head' and 'social_table_tail' are global pointers defining the bounds of the list.
('social_8cc', 'int count_socials()').returns: The total number of social entries in the linked list, as an integer.

('social_8cc', 'void load_social(FILE *f.name: load_social
('social_8cc', 'void load_social(FILE *f.brief: Loads social interaction data from a file into a Social object.
('social_8cc', 'void load_social(FILE *f.details: This function reads various strings from a file and assigns them to the corresponding fields of a Social object. Each field of the Social object represents a different aspect of a social interaction in a game, such as the name of the interaction or the messages displayed to characters and others in different scenarios. The strings are read using the fread_string function, which reads until a specified character is encountered.

params.fp: A pointer to the file from which the social interaction data is to be read.
params.social: A pointer to the Social object where the read data will be stored.

('social_8cc', 'void load_social(FILE *f.returns: This function does not return a value.

('social_8cc', 'void load_social_table().name: load_social_table
('social_8cc', 'void load_social_table().brief: Loads the social interaction data from a file into a linked list of Social objects.
('social_8cc', 'void load_social_table().details:
Loads social interaction data from a file into a linked list of Social objects.
This function opens a predefined file containing social interaction data, reads the number of social interactions, and initializes a doubly linked list with head and tail sentinel nodes. It then iterates over the number of social interactions, creating a new Social object for each, loading data into it from the file, and inserting it into the list. If the file cannot be opened, it logs an error message and exits the function.
('social_8cc', 'void load_social_table().returns: This function does not return a value.

('social_8cc', 'void save_social(const S.name: save_social
('social_8cc', 'void save_social(const S.brief: Saves the details of a Social object to a file stream.
('social_8cc', 'void save_social(const S.details: The function 'save_social' writes the attributes of a Social object to the specified file stream. Each attribute is processed to replace any occurrences of the tilde character '~' with a hyphen '-' before being written. This ensures that the output does not contain any tilde characters, which might be reserved for special purposes in the file format.

params.s: A pointer to a Social object whose details are to be saved.
params.fp: A pointer to a FILE object representing the file stream where the Social object's details will be written.

('social_8cc', 'void save_social(const S.returns: This function does not return a value.

('social_8cc', 'void save_social_table().name: save_social_table
('social_8cc', 'void save_social_table().brief: Saves the social table to a file.
('social_8cc', 'void save_social_table().details: This function opens a file specified by SOCIAL_FILE for writing and saves the details of all social entries from a linked list to this file. It first writes the total number of social entries, then iterates through the list, saving each entry's details using the save_social function. If the file cannot be opened, it logs an error message and exits without saving.
('social_8cc', 'void save_social_table().returns: This function does not return a value.

('social_8cc', 'Social * social_lookup(c.name: social_lookup
('social_8cc', 'Social * social_lookup(c.brief: Finds a social interaction by its name.
('social_8cc', 'Social * social_lookup(c.details: This function searches through a linked list of Social objects to find one with a name that matches the provided String. It starts from the first social in the list and iterates through each element until it finds a match or reaches the end of the list. If a matching Social object is found, it is returned; otherwise, the function returns nullptr.

params.name: The name of the social interaction to search for, provided as a String object.

('social_8cc', 'Social * social_lookup(c.returns: A pointer to the Social object with the matching name, or nullptr if no match is found.

('social_8cc', 'void do_sedit(Character.name: do_sedit
('social_8cc', 'void do_sedit(Character.brief: Handles social editing commands for a character.
('social_8cc', 'void do_sedit(Character.details: The function do_sedit processes various social editing commands issued by a character. It supports creating, deleting, renaming, and displaying social interactions, as well as setting specific messages for different scenarios. The function checks for command validity, ensures no duplicate social names exist, and updates the social table accordingly. It provides feedback to the character through messages sent to their output buffer.

params.ch: A pointer to the Character object issuing the social editing command.
params.argument: A String containing the command and arguments for social editing.

('social_8cc', 'void do_sedit(Character.returns: This function does not return a value.

('remort_8cc', 'int raff_lookup(int inde.name: raff_lookup
('remort_8cc', 'int raff_lookup(int inde.brief: Finds the index of a raff based on its ID.
('remort_8cc', 'int raff_lookup(int inde.details:
Finds the index of a raffect with a given ID.
This function searches through an array of raffects to find a raff with a specified ID. It iterates over the raffects array starting from index 1 up to MAX_RAFFECTS. If a raff with the given ID is found, the function returns its index. If no such raff is found, or if the raff ID is null, the function returns 0.

params.index: The ID of the raffect to search for in the array.

('remort_8cc', 'int raff_lookup(int inde.returns: The index of the raffect with the specified ID, or 0 if no such raffect is found.

('remort_8cc', 'void fix_blank_raff(Char.name: fix_blank_raff
('remort_8cc', 'void fix_blank_raff(Char.brief: Fixes blank remort affects for a character.
('remort_8cc', 'void fix_blank_raff(Char.details: This function iterates through a character's remort affects, starting from a specified index, and removes any zero values except at the end of the list. It is intended for use with non-NPC characters that have undergone a remort process. The function shifts non-zero affects down the list to fill any gaps created by zero values, ensuring that only the last position may contain a zero.

params.ch: A pointer to the Character object whose remort affects are to be fixed.
params.start: The starting index from which to begin checking and fixing remort affects, typically set to 0.

('remort_8cc', 'void fix_blank_raff(Char.returns: This function does not return a value.

('remort_8cc', 'void rem_raff_affect(Cha.name: rem_raff_affect
('remort_8cc', 'void rem_raff_affect(Cha.brief: Removes remort affects from a character based on the affect index.
('remort_8cc', 'void rem_raff_affect(Cha.details: This function removes specific remort affects from a character by modifying their attributes. It checks the affect index to determine the type of affect to remove. If the affect ID is between 900 and 949, it removes a vulnerability affect. If the ID is between 950 and 999, it removes a resistance affect. The function uses the 'affect::remort_affect_modify_char' to apply these changes, passing 'false' to indicate the removal of affects.

params.ch: A pointer to the Character whose remort affects are being modified.
params.index: An integer index used to access the specific remort affect from the 'raffects' array.

('remort_8cc', 'void rem_raff_affect(Cha.returns: This function does not return a value.

('remort_8cc', 'bool HAS_RAFF(Character.name: HAS_RAFF
('remort_8cc', 'bool HAS_RAFF(Character.brief: Checks if a character has a specific remort affect flag.
('remort_8cc', 'bool HAS_RAFF(Character.details: This function determines whether a given character has a specific remort affect flag set. It first checks if the flag is valid (greater than zero) and if the character is not an NPC. If both conditions are met, it iterates through the character's remort affects, which are determined by the character's remort count, to see if the specified flag is present.

params.ch: A pointer to the Character object being checked.
params.flag: The remort affect flag to check for in the character's data.

('remort_8cc', 'bool HAS_RAFF(Character.returns: Returns true if the character has the specified remort affect flag, otherwise returns false.

('remort_8cc', 'bool HAS_RAFF_GROUP(Char.name: HAS_RAFF_GROUP
('remort_8cc', 'bool HAS_RAFF_GROUP(Char.brief: Checks if a character has a specific raffect group.
('remort_8cc', 'bool HAS_RAFF_GROUP(Char.details: This function determines whether a given character possesses a raffect that belongs to a specified group. It first checks if the character is a non-player character (NPC) by calling the 'is_npc' method. If the character is an NPC, the function returns false immediately. For player characters, it iterates through the character's raffects, which are determined by the character's remort count divided by 10 plus one. For each raffect, it uses 'raff_lookup' to find the index of the raffect and checks if its group matches the specified flag. If a match is found, the function returns true.

params.ch: A pointer to the Character object being checked.
params.flag: An integer representing the raffect group to check for.

('remort_8cc', 'bool HAS_RAFF_GROUP(Char.returns: Returns true if the character has a raffect belonging to the specified group, otherwise returns false.

('remort_8cc', 'void raff_add_to_char(Ch.name: raff_add_to_char
('remort_8cc', 'void raff_add_to_char(Ch.brief: Adds a remort affect to a character based on a given affect ID.
('remort_8cc', 'void raff_add_to_char(Ch.details: This function attempts to add a remort affect to a character by looking up the affect ID in a predefined list of remort affects. If the affect ID is valid and the affect has associated attributes to add, it modifies the character's attributes accordingly. The function distinguishes between vulnerability and resistance affects based on the ID range and applies the appropriate modification.

params.ch: A pointer to the Character object to which the remort affect will be added.
params.raff_id: An integer representing the remort affect ID to be added to the character.

('remort_8cc', 'void raff_add_to_char(Ch.returns: This function does not return a value.

('remort_8cc', 'void roll_one_raff(Chara.name: roll_one_raff
('remort_8cc', 'void roll_one_raff(Chara.brief: Assigns a random remort affect to a character.
('remort_8cc', 'void roll_one_raff(Chara.details: This function selects a random remort affect from a predefined set and assigns it to a specified position in a victim character's remort affect array. The selection process involves generating random numbers and checking conditions based on the remort affect's ID and chance, as well as the victim's remort count. The function ensures that the selected affect is not already present in the victim's affects and does not belong to a group that the victim already has. If the character receiving the affect is different from the character initiating the function, a message is sent to both characters indicating the addition of the affect.

params.ch: A pointer to the Character object initiating the remort affect assignment.
params.victim: A pointer to the Character object that will receive the remort affect.
params.place: An integer representing the index in the victim's remort affect array where the new affect will be stored.

('remort_8cc', 'void roll_one_raff(Chara.returns: This function does not return a value.

('remort_8cc', 'void roll_raffects(Chara.name: roll_raffects
('remort_8cc', 'void roll_raffects(Chara.brief: Assigns multiple random remort affects to a character.
('remort_8cc', 'void roll_raffects(Chara.details: This function iterates over a calculated number of slots in the victim's remort affect array and assigns a random remort affect to each slot. The number of affects assigned is determined by dividing the victim's remort count by 10 and adding 1. It is assumed that the function is used in contexts where both ch and victim are player characters, with ch being an immortal character and victim being a character undergoing remort.

params.ch: A pointer to the Character object initiating the remort affect assignment.
params.victim: A pointer to the Character object that will receive the remort affects.

('remort_8cc', 'void roll_raffects(Chara.returns: This function does not return a value.

('remort_8cc', 'bool HAS_EXTRACLASS(Char.name: HAS_EXTRACLASS
('remort_8cc', 'bool HAS_EXTRACLASS(Char.brief: Checks if a character has access to an extra class skill.
('remort_8cc', 'bool HAS_EXTRACLASS(Char.details: The function determines whether a given character has access to a specific extra class skill. It first checks if the skill type is 'unknown' or if the character is a non-player character (NPC), returning false in these cases. For player characters, it calculates the number of extra class slots available based on the character's remort count and checks if the specified skill type is present in the character's extra class skills.

params.ch: A pointer to the Character object being checked.
params.sn: The skill::type enumeration value representing the skill to check for.

('remort_8cc', 'bool HAS_EXTRACLASS(Char.returns: Returns true if the character has the specified extra class skill, otherwise returns false.

('remort_8cc', 'bool CAN_USE_RSKILL(Char.name: CAN_USE_RSKILL
('remort_8cc', 'bool CAN_USE_RSKILL(Char.brief: Determines if a character can use a remort skill.
('remort_8cc', 'bool CAN_USE_RSKILL(Char.details: This function checks whether a given character is allowed to use a specified remort skill. It first checks if the character is a non-player character (NPC) and if the skill is recognized. For player characters, it verifies if the character is immortal, a remort, and belongs to a guild. It also checks if the character has the required skill level and if the skill belongs to the character's guild or is an extra class skill.

params.ch: A pointer to the Character object being checked for skill usage.
params.sn: The skill::type enumeration value representing the skill to be checked.

('remort_8cc', 'bool CAN_USE_RSKILL(Char.returns: Returns true if the character can use the specified remort skill, otherwise returns false.

('remort_8cc', 'void list_extraskill(Cha.name: list_extraskill
('remort_8cc', 'void list_extraskill(Cha.brief: Displays a list of extraclass remort skills for a character.
('remort_8cc', 'void list_extraskill(Cha.details: This function generates and sends a formatted list of extraclass remort skills available to a character, excluding those from the character's own guild unless the character is immortal. It iterates over all guilds and skills, checking conditions such as the character's immortality status and skill usability. The formatted list is then sent to the character's output buffer for display.

params.ch: A pointer to the Character object for whom the extraclass remort skills are being listed.

('remort_8cc', 'void list_extraskill(Cha.returns: This function does not return a value.

('remort_8cc', 'void do_eremort(Characte.name: do_eremort
('remort_8cc', 'void do_eremort(Characte.brief: Handles the process of assigning an extraclass remort skill to a character.
('remort_8cc', 'void do_eremort(Characte.details: The function do_eremort allows a character to gain an extraclass remort skill, provided they meet certain conditions. It first checks if the character is an NPC or an immortal, in which case it lists available extraclass skills or sends a confused message. If the character has no remort count, it sends a confused message. If no argument is provided, it lists the character's current extraclass skills. If a skill name is provided, it verifies if the skill is valid, a remort skill, outside the player's class, and not barred from the class. It also checks if the character already knows the skill and has enough training points. If all conditions are met, the skill is added to the character's extraclass skills, and training points are deducted. If the character already has the maximum number of extraclass skills, an appropriate message is sent.

params.ch: A pointer to the Character object attempting to gain an extraclass remort skill.
params.argument: A String object containing the skill name or command argument provided by the character.

('remort_8cc', 'void do_eremort(Characte.returns: This function does not return a value.

('remort_8cc', 'void do_remort(Character.name: do_remort
('remort_8cc', 'void do_remort(Character.brief: Handles the remort process for a character, allowing them to be reborn with new attributes.
('remort_8cc', 'void do_remort(Character.details: The function do_remort facilitates the remort process for a character in the game, allowing them to be reborn with potentially new race, deity, and title. The function checks various conditions such as the character's level, equipment status, and remort eligibility before proceeding. If valid, it resets the character's attributes, removes all affects, and applies new racial affects. The character's experience and level are reset, and any associated pet is also adjusted. The function concludes by announcing the remort to the game world.

params.ch: A pointer to the Character object that is executing the remort command.
params.argument: A String containing the arguments for the remort command, including the target character and optional race, deity, and title.

('remort_8cc', 'void do_remort(Character.returns: This function does not return a value.

('_player_8hh', 'long get_pc_id()').name: get_pc_id
('_player_8hh', 'long get_pc_id()').brief: Retrieves the unique identifier for the current PC.
('_player_8hh', 'long get_pc_id()').details: This function returns a long integer that uniquely identifies the personal computer on which the function is executed. The identifier is typically derived from hardware or system-specific attributes, ensuring that it is unique to the machine. This can be used for licensing, tracking, or system-specific configurations.
('_player_8hh', 'long get_pc_id()').returns: A long integer representing the unique identifier of the current PC.

('departed_8cc', 'void load_departed_lis.name: load_departed_list
('departed_8cc', 'void load_departed_lis.brief: Loads the list of departed players from a file.
('departed_8cc', 'void load_departed_lis.details: This function initializes a linked list to store departed players and attempts to open a file specified by DEPARTED_FILE to read the names of departed players. It reads each name from the file until a line starting with '#' is encountered, inserting each name into the list using the insert_departed function. If the file cannot be opened, a bug is logged, and the function exits without modifying the list.
('departed_8cc', 'void load_departed_lis.returns: This function does not return a value.

('departed_8cc', 'void save_departed_lis.name: save_departed_list
('departed_8cc', 'void save_departed_lis.brief: Saves the list of departed players to a file.
('departed_8cc', 'void save_departed_lis.details: This function writes the names of departed players to a file specified by DEPARTED_FILE. It opens the file in write mode and iterates through the list of departed players, writing each player's name followed by a newline character. If the file cannot be opened, it logs a bug message and exits the function. After writing all player names, it writes a '#' character followed by a newline to signify the end of the list, and then closes the file.
('departed_8cc', 'void save_departed_lis.returns: This function does not return a value.

('departed_8cc', 'void insert_departed(c.name: insert_departed
('departed_8cc', 'void insert_departed(c.brief: Inserts a new departed player into a sorted list.
('departed_8cc', 'void insert_departed(c.details:
Inserts a departed player into a sorted list based on their name.
This function adds a new DepartedPlayer object, initialized with the given name, into a doubly linked list of departed players. The list is sorted in case-insensitive lexicographical order based on player names. The function traverses the list to find the correct insertion point for the new player, ensuring the list remains sorted. If the list is empty or the new player should be inserted at the end, the function handles these cases appropriately.

params.name: The name of the departed player to be inserted into the list.

('departed_8cc', 'void insert_departed(c.returns: This function does not return a value.

('departed_8cc', 'void remove_departed(c.name: remove_departed
('departed_8cc', 'void remove_departed(c.brief: Removes a departed player from the list by name.
('departed_8cc', 'void remove_departed(c.details: This function searches through a linked list of departed players and removes the player whose name matches the given name, ignoring case. It updates the pointers of the adjacent nodes to maintain the list structure and deallocates the memory for the removed player.

params.name: The name of the departed player to be removed from the list.

('departed_8cc', 'void remove_departed(c.returns: This function does not return a value.

('departed_8cc', 'bool has_departed(cons.name: has_departed
('departed_8cc', 'bool has_departed(cons.brief: Checks if a player with the given name has departed.
('departed_8cc', 'bool has_departed(cons.details: This function iterates through a linked list of departed players to determine if a player with the specified name exists in the list. It performs a case-insensitive comparison of the given name against each player's name in the list using the strcasecmp function.

params.name: The name of the player to check for in the departed players list.

('departed_8cc', 'bool has_departed(cons.returns: Returns true if a player with the given name is found in the departed list, otherwise returns false.

('objstate_8cc', 'cJSON * fwrite_obj(Obj.name: fwrite_obj
('objstate_8cc', 'cJSON * fwrite_obj(Obj.brief: Serializes a game object and its contents into a JSON object.
('objstate_8cc', 'cJSON * fwrite_obj(Obj.details:
Serializes a game object into a JSON representation.
The function fwrite_obj takes a game object represented by the Object structure and serializes its attributes and contents into a JSON object using the cJSON library. It compares the current state of the object's attributes with their default values from the object's index data and only includes those attributes in the JSON object that differ from their defaults. Additionally, it checks if the object is enchanted and includes its affect list if applicable. Extra descriptions, flags, and other relevant attributes are also serialized. The function handles nested objects by recursively serializing contained objects and gems.

params.obj: A pointer to the Object structure to be serialized into a JSON object.

('objstate_8cc', 'cJSON * fwrite_obj(Obj.returns: A pointer to a cJSON object representing the serialized state of the input game object, or NULL if memory allocation fails.

('objstate_8cc', 'Object * fread_obj(cJS.name: fread_obj
('objstate_8cc', 'Object * fread_obj(cJS.brief: Reads and initializes a game object from a JSON representation.
('objstate_8cc', 'Object * fread_obj(cJS.details: This function processes a JSON object to create and initialize a game object, potentially including its contents. It first attempts to retrieve the object prototype using a virtual number (vnum) from the JSON data. If the prototype is found, a new object is created based on this prototype. If not, or if the vnum is missing, a dummy object is created. The function then iterates over the JSON data to set various attributes of the object, such as affects, description, and contained items. Special handling is included for weapon flags and affects based on the version of the object format.

params.json: A pointer to the cJSON object representing the game object to be read.
params.version: An integer representing the version of the object format, which influences how certain fields are processed.

('objstate_8cc', 'Object * fread_obj(cJS.returns: A pointer to the newly created and initialized Object, or a dummy Object if the prototype could not be found.

('objstate_8cc', 'bool has_modified_cont.name: has_modified_contents
('objstate_8cc', 'bool has_modified_cont.brief: Checks if an object contains modified contents.
('objstate_8cc', 'bool has_modified_cont.details: This function iterates over the contents of the given object to determine if any of the contained objects do not match their expected 'put' reset state. It returns true if any contained object is either missing a reset, has a reset command other than 'P', or has a reset argument that does not match the parent object's index data. This is used to identify objects with contents that have been altered from their default reset state.

params.obj: A pointer to the Object whose contents are to be checked for modifications.

('objstate_8cc', 'bool has_modified_cont.returns: Returns true if the object contains any modified contents that do not match their 'put' reset state; otherwise, returns false.

('objstate_8cc', 'bool is_worth_saving(O.name: is_worth_saving
('objstate_8cc', 'bool is_worth_saving(O.brief: Determines if an object is worth saving to the list.
('objstate_8cc', 'bool is_worth_saving(O.details: This function evaluates whether a given object should be saved based on its location, type, and state. It checks if the object is in a room and not carried or contained within another object. It excludes certain item types such as potions, scrolls, and NPC corpses, which typically have timers or are not worth saving. Additionally, it ensures that objects of a high level or those with active timers are not saved, unless they are player corpses. If the object resets on the ground, it is only saved if its contents have been modified.

params.obj: A pointer to the Object being evaluated for saving.

('objstate_8cc', 'bool is_worth_saving(O.returns: Returns true if the object is deemed worth saving based on its location, type, and state; otherwise, returns false.

('objstate_8cc', 'int objstate_save_item.name: objstate_save_items
('objstate_8cc', 'int objstate_save_item.brief: Saves all objects lying on the ground to disk.
('objstate_8cc', 'int objstate_save_item.details:
Saves the current state of game objects to a JSON file.
This function iterates through the list of objects in the game world and saves those that are deemed worth saving to a JSON file on disk. It creates a JSON representation of the objects, writes this data to a temporary file, and then renames the file to a permanent location. This function is called occasionally from update_handler and selectively from some parts of get_obj and drop_obj.
('objstate_8cc', 'int objstate_save_item.returns: Returns 0 upon completion, indicating the function executed without errors.

('objstate_8cc', 'int count_items(const.name: count_items
('objstate_8cc', 'int count_items(const.brief: Counts the total number of items contained within an object.
('objstate_8cc', 'int count_items(const.details: This function recursively counts the number of objects contained within the given object, including the object itself. It traverses the linked list of contained objects, summing up the counts of all nested objects.

params.obj: A pointer to the Object whose items are to be counted. It is expected to have a 'contains' pointer to the first contained object and a 'next_content' pointer for linked list traversal.

('objstate_8cc', 'int count_items(const.returns: The total count of the object itself and all objects contained within it.

('objstate_8cc', 'int objstate_load_item.name: objstate_load_items
('objstate_8cc', 'int objstate_load_item.brief: Loads and initializes game objects from a JSON file into their respective rooms.
('objstate_8cc', 'int objstate_load_item.details:
Loads game objects from a JSON file and places them into their respective rooms.
This function reads a JSON file containing serialized game objects, parses it, and initializes these objects into their respective rooms within the game world. It first constructs the filename for the JSON file and reads its contents into a cJSON object. The function checks for a version number within the JSON data and retrieves the list of objects. For each object, it determines the room location and attempts to retrieve the corresponding Room object. If the room exists, it reads the object data and checks if an object with the same prototype already exists in the room. If so, the existing object is removed and replaced with the newly loaded one. Special handling is performed for donation pits, which are logged and stored in a global reference if not already set. The function counts the total number of items loaded and returns this count.
('objstate_8cc', 'int objstate_load_item.returns: The total number of objects and their contents loaded into the game world.

('dispel_8cc', 'bool saves_spell(int lev.name: saves_spell
('dispel_8cc', 'bool saves_spell(int lev.brief: Computes whether a character successfully makes a saving throw against a spell.
('dispel_8cc', 'bool saves_spell(int lev.details:
Determines if a character successfully saves against a spell.
This function calculates the probability of a character (victim) successfully making a saving throw against a spell cast by another character. The saving throw is influenced by the level difference between the victim and the spell caster, the victim's saving throw attributes, and any active affects such as berserk. Additionally, the victim's defense modifier against the specific damage type of the spell is considered. The final computed chance is clamped between 5% and 95% before determining if the saving throw is successful.

params.level: The level of the spell caster, used to calculate the level difference for the save chance.
params.victim: A pointer to the Character object representing the target of the spell, whose save chance is being calculated.
params.dam_type: The type of damage the spell inflicts, used to determine the victim's defense modifier.

('dispel_8cc', 'bool saves_spell(int lev.returns: Returns true if the character successfully saves against the spell, otherwise returns false.

('dispel_8cc', 'bool check_dispel_char(i.name: check_dispel_char
('dispel_8cc', 'bool check_dispel_char(i.argsstring: (int dis_level, Character *victim, affect::type type, bool save)
('dispel_8cc', 'bool check_dispel_char(i.brief: Attempts to dispel all affects of a specified type from a character based on dispel level and saving throw.
('dispel_8cc', 'bool check_dispel_char(i.details:
Checks and attempts to dispel a specific affect from a character based on dispel level and saving throw.
This function tries to remove all affects of a given type from the target character ('victim') by applying dispel logic with the specified dispel level ('dis_level') and save option ('save'). It initializes a set of parameters ('dispel_params') and invokes 'affect::iterate_over_char' with 'affect_fn_dispel_char' to mark affects for removal based on the dispel criteria. If any affects are marked (indicated by 'params.count' > 0), it proceeds to remove them from the character's affect list using 'affect::remove_marked_from_char'. After removal, if the affect type no longer exists on the character, it retrieves the corresponding table entry via 'affect::lookup' and sends appropriate messages to the room and the victim, if message strings are defined. The function returns true if any affects were dispelled, otherwise false.

params.dis_level: The level of the dispel attempt, influencing success chances.
params.victim: Pointer to the Character object from whom the affect is to be dispelled.
params.type: The affect::type enumeration value indicating which affect to dispel.
params.save: Boolean indicating whether a saving throw is required for the dispel to succeed.

('dispel_8cc', 'bool check_dispel_char(i.returns: Returns true if an affect was successfully dispelled from the character; otherwise, false.

('dispel_8cc', 'bool check_dispel_obj(in.name: check_dispel_obj
('dispel_8cc', 'bool check_dispel_obj(in.argsstring: (int dis_level, Object *obj, affect::type type, bool save)
('dispel_8cc', 'bool check_dispel_obj(in.brief: Attempts to dispel affects on an object based on specified conditions.
('dispel_8cc', 'bool check_dispel_obj(in.details:
Attempts to dispel affects of a specified type from an object.
This function checks and attempts to dispel affects on a given object by iterating over its affect list. It uses the specified dispel level, affect type, and save flag to determine which affects should be marked for removal. If any affects are marked, they are removed from the object's affect list. The function returns true if any affects were dispelled, otherwise false.

params.dis_level: The level of the dispel operation, which influences the success of dispelling affects.
params.obj: A pointer to the Object whose affects are to be checked and potentially dispelled.
params.type: The type of affect to be targeted for dispelling.
params.save: A boolean flag indicating whether a saving throw should be considered in the dispel operation.

('dispel_8cc', 'bool check_dispel_obj(in.returns: Returns true if any affects were successfully marked and removed; otherwise, returns false.

('dispel_8cc', 'bool undo_spell(int dis_.name: undo_spell
('dispel_8cc', 'bool undo_spell(int dis_.argsstring: (int dis_level, Character *victim, affect::type type, bool save)
('dispel_8cc', 'bool undo_spell(int dis_.brief: Attempts to undo or dispel a specific affect of a given type from a character if the affect is marked as undoable.
('dispel_8cc', 'bool undo_spell(int dis_.details:
Attempts to undo (remove) affects of a specified type from a character if permitted.
This function checks whether the specified affect type can be undone by consulting the dispel table. If the affect type is found and marked as undoable, it proceeds to call check_dispel_char to attempt to dispel all affects of that type from the victim, considering the dispel level and saving throw. The function returns true if the dispel attempt was successful, indicating that the affects were removed; otherwise, it returns false. If the affect type is not found or not marked as undoable, no dispel attempt is made, and false is returned.

params.dis_level: The level of the dispel attempt, influencing the chance of success.
params.victim: Pointer to the Character object from whom affects are to be potentially removed.
params.type: The specific affect::type to be targeted for undoing.
params.save: Boolean indicating whether to consider a saving throw during the dispel attempt.

('dispel_8cc', 'bool undo_spell(int dis_.returns: A boolean value indicating whether any affects of the specified type were successfully dispelled (true) or not (false).

('dispel_8cc', 'bool dispel_char(Charact.name: dispel_char
('dispel_8cc', 'bool dispel_char(Charact.brief: Dispel a list of spells from a character using dispel magic or cancellation.
('dispel_8cc', 'bool dispel_char(Charact.details:
Attempts to dispel all effects of specified types from a character based on dispel level and cancellation status.
This function attempts to dispel multiple spell affects from the specified victim character based on the provided level and whether the dispel is a cancellation. It iterates through a predefined dispel table, applying dispel or cancellation logic depending on the 'cancellation' flag. The function returns true if any affects of the spells were successfully dispelled or canceled; otherwise, it returns false.

params.victim: Pointer to the Character object from whom effects are to be dispelled or canceled.
params.level: The dispel or cancellation level influencing success chances.
params.cancellation: Boolean indicating whether the operation is a cancellation (true) or a dispel (false).

('dispel_8cc', 'bool dispel_char(Charact.returns: A boolean value indicating whether any effects of the specified types were successfully dispelled or canceled (true) or not (false).

('dispel_8cc', 'bool level_save(int dis_.name: level_save
('dispel_8cc', 'bool level_save(int dis_.brief: Determines if a saving throw is successful based on level difference.
('dispel_8cc', 'bool level_save(int dis_.details:
Determines if a level-based saving throw is successful.
This function calculates a saving throw chance based on the difference between a character's saving level and the difficulty level. It computes a save percentage, ensuring it is within the range of 5 to 95, and then uses this percentage to determine if the saving throw is successful by calling roll_chance.

params.dis_level: The difficulty level of the challenge.
params.save_level: The saving level of the entity attempting the save.

('dispel_8cc', 'bool level_save(int dis_.returns: Returns true if the saving throw is successful, indicating the entity overcomes the challenge; otherwise, returns false.
