('handler_8cc', 'void destroy_obj(Object.name: destroy_obj
('handler_8cc', 'void destroy_obj(Object.brief: Destroys a game object and handles its contents.
('handler_8cc', 'void destroy_obj(Object.details:
Destroys an object while preserving its contents.
This function is responsible for destroying a game object by first distributing its contents and gems to appropriate locations using the spill_contents function. After all contents have been handled, the object itself is removed from the game world using the extract_obj function. This ensures that no resources or items are lost when an object is destroyed.

params.obj: A pointer to the Object that is to be destroyed. The contents of this object will be preserved.

('handler_8cc', 'void destroy_obj(Object.returns: This function does not return a value.

('handler_8cc', 'void extract_obj(Object.name: extract_obj
('handler_8cc', 'void extract_obj(Object.brief: Removes an object and its contents from the game world.
('handler_8cc', 'void extract_obj(Object.details:
Removes an object from the game world.
The function extract_obj is responsible for completely removing an object from the game world. It first determines the current location of the object, whether it is in a room, carried by a character, inside another object, in a locker, or in a strongbox, and removes it from that location. It then recursively removes any objects contained within the object. After handling the contents, it removes the object from the global object list maintained by the game world. If the object is the donation pit, it sets the donation pit to null. Finally, it decrements the count of the object's index data and deletes the object.

params.obj: A pointer to the Object that is to be removed from the game world.

('handler_8cc', 'void extract_obj(Object.returns: This function does not return a value.

('_object_8cc', 'void unique_item(Object.name: unique_item
('_object_8cc', 'void unique_item(Object.brief: Modifies an object to potentially make it unique by altering its attributes.
('_object_8cc', 'void unique_item(Object.details:
Modifies an item to potentially make it unique by altering its attributes.
The function 'unique_item' attempts to apply random modifications to an Object to make it unique. It first checks if a random chance condition is met before proceeding. If the object type is not among a predefined list of types to ignore, the function may apply random attribute changes such as modifying its stats, values, condition, weight, or cost. The modifications are probabilistic, with different chances for each type of change. The function ensures that certain conditions are met before making changes, such as the item being takeable or wearable. If changes are made, they are logged for testing purposes.

params.item: A pointer to the Object that is being considered for modification to become unique.

('_object_8cc', 'void unique_item(Object.returns: This function does not return a value.

('structconn_1_1_read_new_m_o_t_d_state'.name: handleInput
('structconn_1_1_read_new_m_o_t_d_state'.brief: Handles input for the new Message of the Day (MOTD) state, initializing character data and transitioning to the appropriate game state.
('structconn_1_1_read_new_m_o_t_d_state'.details: This function processes input received while a character is in the ReadNewMOTDState. It sets the character's text color, checks for null passwords, adds the character to the game world, initializes new characters with default attributes, assigns starting equipment, places the character in an appropriate room based on their status, notifies other players of their entry, displays the initial look command, logs the login event, manages pet placement, displays unread messages, and sets the character's rating and backup status. It also configures VT100 terminal settings if enabled. The function ultimately transitions the character to the 'playing' state.

params.d: Pointer to the Descriptor object representing the connection and associated character.
params.argument: Input string received from the user, not directly used in this implementation.

('structconn_1_1_read_new_m_o_t_d_state'.returns: Returns a pointer to the State object representing the next state, which is the 'playing' state.

('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'worldmap::.name: Region
('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'worldmap::.brief: Constructs a Region object by loading map data and configuration from a file stream.
('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'worldmap::.details: This constructor initializes a Region instance using data read from a provided FILE pointer. It loads an associated image based on a filename read from the file, sets alignment coordinates, and processes subsequent characters to configure allowed mob and room resets, as well as color mappings. It also performs validation to detect duplicate color definitions, logging warnings when such duplicates are found. The function modifies internal data structures to represent the region's properties based on the file contents.

params.area: Reference to an Area object associated with this region.
params.fp: Pointer to the FILE stream from which region configuration data is read.

('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'worldmap::.returns: This constructor does not return a value; it initializes the Region object based on file data.

('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'virtual wo.name: ~Region
('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'virtual wo.details: This is the virtual destructor for the Region class. It ensures that derived class destructors are called correctly when an object is deleted through a pointer to the base class. This is important for proper resource management and cleanup in polymorphic base classes.

('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'unsigned i.name: align_x
('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'unsigned i.details: This function returns the current horizontal alignment value stored in the private member variable '_align_x'. It is a constant member function, indicating that it does not modify any member variables of the class.
('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'unsigned i.returns: An unsigned integer representing the horizontal alignment value.

('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'unsigned i.name: align_y
('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'unsigned i.details: This function returns the current vertical alignment value stored in the private member variable '_align_y'. It is a constant member function, indicating that it does not modify any member variables of the class.
('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'unsigned i.returns: An unsigned integer representing the vertical alignment value.

('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'const MapC.name: vnum_to_color
('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'const MapC.brief: Retrieves the color associated with a given virtual number (vnum).
('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'const MapC.details: This function looks up the color associated with a specified Vnum in the region's prototype color map. If the Vnum is found, it returns the corresponding MapColor. If the Vnum is not found, it returns a default uncolored MapColor.

params.vnum: The virtual number (Vnum) for which the associated MapColor is to be retrieved.

('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'const MapC.returns: A reference to the MapColor associated with the given Vnum, or a default uncolored MapColor if the Vnum is not found.

('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'const Vnum.name: color_to_vnum
('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'const Vnum.brief: Converts a MapColor to its corresponding Vnum.
('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'const Vnum.details: This function takes a MapColor object and attempts to find its corresponding Vnum in the _prototype_color_map. If the color is MapColor::uncolored, it returns 0, indicating no room is associated with that color. If the color is found in the map, the corresponding Vnum is returned. If the color is not found, it returns -1, indicating an error or that the pixel color does not correspond to any known Vnum.

params.color: The MapColor object to be converted to a Vnum.

('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'const Vnum.returns: Returns the Vnum corresponding to the given MapColor. Returns 0 if the color is MapColor::uncolored, and -1 if the color does not correspond to any Vnum.

('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'const MapC.name: get_color
('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'const MapC.argsstring: (unsigned int x, unsigned int y) const
('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'const MapC.brief: Retrieves the color of a pixel at specified coordinates within the region.
('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'const MapC.details: This function checks the alpha channel of the pixel at the given (x, y) coordinates in the region's image. If the alpha value is greater than zero, it constructs and returns a MapColor object using the red, green, and blue channel values at the same coordinates. If the alpha value is zero or the coordinates are out of bounds, it returns a default uncolored MapColor.

params.x: The x-coordinate of the pixel within the region.
params.y: The y-coordinate of the pixel within the region.

('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'const MapC.returns: A MapColor object representing the RGB color of the pixel if it is visible (alpha > 0), or MapColor::uncolored if the pixel is not visible or the coordinates are invalid.

('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'const Vnum.name: get_vnum
('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'const Vnum.argsstring: (unsigned int x, unsigned int y) const
('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'const Vnum.brief: Retrieves the Vnum for a pixel at specified coordinates within the region.
('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'const Vnum.details: This function determines the Vnum associated with a pixel located at the given x and y coordinates within the region. It first retrieves the color of the pixel using the get_color function. Then, it converts this color to a Vnum using the color_to_vnum function. If the pixel is not visible or the coordinates are invalid, the function returns 0. If the color does not correspond to any Vnum, it returns -1.

params.x: The x-coordinate of the pixel within the region.
params.y: The y-coordinate of the pixel within the region.

('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'const Vnum.returns: The Vnum corresponding to the color of the pixel at the specified coordinates. Returns 0 if the pixel is not visible or the coordinates are invalid, and -1 if the color does not correspond to any Vnum.

('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'void world.name: create_rooms
('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'void world.brief: Creates room objects within the region based on pixel colors and associated prototypes.
('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'void world.details: This function iterates over each pixel in the region's image, retrieves its color, and determines the corresponding Vnum. If the Vnum is valid and a matching prototype exists, it creates a new Room object based on the prototype, assigns its location based on the pixel coordinates adjusted by region alignment, and adds it to the area's room collection. The room is also inserted into the global map tree for spatial indexing. The function logs warnings for undefined or missing prototypes and enforces a maximum room count per prototype, terminating the program if exceeded.

params.region: The current Region object invoking the function, which contains image data, area, and other relevant properties.

('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'void world.returns: This function has no return value; it modifies the area's room collection and the global map tree by creating and inserting new Room objects.

('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'worldmap::.name: Region
('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'worldmap::.brief: Copy constructor for the Region class.
('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'worldmap::.details: Creates a new instance of the Region class by copying the state of an existing Region object. This constructor duplicates the data and state of the provided Region object, ensuring that the new instance is a separate copy with the same initial values.

('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'Region & w.name: operator=
('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'Region & w.brief: Assigns the values from another Region object to this object.
('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'Region & w.details: This copy assignment operator takes another Region object as its parameter and copies its contents into the current object. It ensures that the current object becomes a duplicate of the provided Region object, managing any necessary resource allocation or deallocation. This operator handles self-assignment gracefully.

params.rhs: The Region object whose values are to be copied.

('classworldmap_1_1_region', 'Region & w.returns: A reference to the current object after assignment.

('fight_8cc', 'void check_assist(Charact.name: check_assist
('fight_8cc', 'void check_assist(Charact.brief: Checks and initiates automatic assistance behavior among characters in a room based on various group and flag conditions.
('fight_8cc', 'void check_assist(Charact.details: This function iterates through all characters present in the same room as the character 'ch', evaluating whether each character should assist in combat against the 'victim'. Assistance is determined based on whether characters are awake, not already fighting, and meet specific criteria such as group membership, NPC or player status, and active flags like auto-assist or assist-all. The function considers various flags and affect conditions, including charm effects and safety checks, to decide if an assist should occur. When conditions are met, the assisting character may emote and then perform multiple attack hits on the victim.

params.ch: Pointer to the character for whom assistance checks are being performed.
params.victim: Pointer to the character who is the target of potential assistance.

('fight_8cc', 'void check_assist(Charact.returns: Void; the function performs side effects such as initiating combat and emotes without returning a value.

('fight_8cc', 'void check_cond(Character.name: check_cond
('fight_8cc', 'void check_cond(Character.brief: Checks and applies damage to an object based on character effects and conditions, potentially destroying it.
('fight_8cc', 'void check_cond(Character.details: This function evaluates whether a given character can damage a specified object during combat. It first verifies that the character is not an immortal and that the object has a valid condition value. If the character has the 'sheen' affect, the function exits early, as sheen provides complete protection. Otherwise, it randomly determines whether damage is applied, with a 1 in 501 chance. If damage occurs, the object's condition is reduced, with additional damage if the character has the 'steel_mist' affect. When the object's condition drops to zero or below, it is destroyed, and appropriate messages are displayed, including scattering of contents and gems if the object is not in a duel arena. The function does not return a value but modifies the state of the object and possibly the game environment.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character object performing the action, used to check effects and display messages.
params.obj: Pointer to the Object object being damaged and potentially destroyed.

('fight_8cc', 'void check_cond(Character.returns: This function does not return a value; it modifies the object's condition and may destroy the object.

('fight_8cc', 'void check_all_cond(Chara.name: check_all_cond
('fight_8cc', 'void check_all_cond(Chara.brief: Checks and updates the condition of all equipment worn by a character, unless certain effects are active.
('fight_8cc', 'void check_all_cond(Chara.details: This function iterates through all equipment slots of the specified character, verifying the presence and condition of each item. If an item exists and its condition is valid, it invokes 'check_cond' to assess and potentially modify the item's state, which may include destroying the item. The function skips processing if the character is an NPC, is immortal, or has the 'sheen' affect active, ensuring only relevant characters have their equipment conditions checked.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character object whose equipment conditions are to be checked.

('fight_8cc', 'void check_all_cond(Chara.returns: Void; the function performs in-place checks and updates without returning a value.

('fight_8cc', 'void check_killer(Charact.name: check_killer
('fight_8cc', 'void check_killer(Charact.brief: Checks and updates a character's status to become a killer based on combat interactions and game conditions.
('fight_8cc', 'void check_killer(Charact.details:
Checks whether an attack by a character justifies assigning the KILLER flag to them.
This function evaluates whether a character (ch) should be flagged as a killer after an attack on a victim. It first resolves charm effects to identify the true attacker, then applies several game rules to determine if the attack is considered hostile, including NPC status, player flags (killer, thief), arena conditions, ongoing wars, and quest area flags. If the attack qualifies as hostile and the attacker is not immune or already flagged, the function sets the attacker’s killer status, updates relevant flags, saves the character state, and notifies the attacker. It also handles special cases such as charm effects, NPCs, immortals, and recent combat conditions to prevent false positives.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character object representing the attacker.
params.victim: Pointer to the Character object representing the target of the attack.

('fight_8cc', 'void check_killer(Charact.returns: Void; the function performs side effects such as setting flags, removing affects, and logging, without returning a value.

('fight_8cc', 'bool check_dodge(Characte.name: check_dodge
('fight_8cc', 'bool check_dodge(Characte.brief: Checks whether a character successfully dodges an attack, considering various factors and possibly triggering a defensive heal.
('fight_8cc', 'bool check_dodge(Characte.details: This function evaluates if the 'victim' character successfully dodges an attack from 'ch' based on their dodge skill level, size, attributes, active effects (such as haste or slow), visibility, and level difference. It incorporates randomness via a chance roll and adjusts the dodge probability accordingly. If the dodge succeeds, it outputs appropriate messages to both characters, possibly triggers a healing effect if certain conditions are met, and updates skill improvement. The function returns true if the dodge occurs, false otherwise. It also accounts for special effects like paralysis, and logs bugs if attack types are invalid.

params.ch: Pointer to the attacking Character object.
params.victim: Pointer to the defending Character object attempting to dodge.
params.attack_skill: The skill::type representing the attack's skill type, used for damage message lookup.

('fight_8cc', 'bool check_dodge(Characte.returns: Boolean value indicating whether the dodge was successful (true) or not (false).

('fight_8cc', 'bool check_blur(Character.name: check_blur
('fight_8cc', 'bool check_blur(Character.brief: Checks whether a character successfully evades an attack using the 'blur' skill, applying visual and healing effects if successful.
('fight_8cc', 'bool check_blur(Character.details: This function evaluates the chance for a victim character to evade an attack through the 'blur' skill, considering factors such as paralysis, skill level, size, attributes, speed effects, visibility, and level differences. It computes a chance percentage, compares it against a random roll, and determines success or failure. On success, it displays appropriate messages to the attacker and victim, and may trigger a healing effect if the victim has a specific grace attribute. The function also attempts to improve the victim's skill proficiency based on the outcome. It returns true if the evasion is successful, otherwise false.

params.ch: Pointer to the attacker character attempting the attack.
params.victim: Pointer to the defending character attempting to evade via blur.
params.attack_skill: The skill type used for the attack, influencing the damage message.

('fight_8cc', 'bool check_blur(Character.returns: Boolean value indicating whether the victim successfully evaded the attack using blur (true) or not (false).

('fight_8cc', 'bool check_shblock(Charac.name: check_shblock
('fight_8cc', 'bool check_shblock(Charac.brief: Checks if a character successfully blocks an attack with a shield and applies related effects.
('fight_8cc', 'bool check_shblock(Charac.details: This function determines whether the victim character successfully performs a shield block against an attack from another character. It considers the victim's shield skill level, current effects such as paralysis, and compares a calculated chance against a random percentage. If successful, it displays appropriate messages, restores some health or mana based on attributes, and may trigger additional effects like healing from Montrey's Grace. It also updates the victim's condition and skill improvement status accordingly. The function returns true if the shield block occurs, otherwise false.

params.ch: Pointer to the attacking Character object.
params.victim: Pointer to the defending Character object attempting to block.
params.attack_skill: The skill::type representing the attack's skill type; used for damage message lookup.

('fight_8cc', 'bool check_shblock(Charac.returns: Boolean value indicating whether the shield block was successful (true) or not (false).

('fight_8cc', 'bool check_parry(Characte.name: check_parry
('fight_8cc', 'bool check_parry(Characte.brief: Checks whether a character successfully parries an attack and executes related effects.
('fight_8cc', 'bool check_parry(Characte.details: This function determines if the victim character successfully parries an incoming attack based on their skill level, equipped weapon, visibility, and level difference. It accounts for special conditions such as paralysis effects, weapon types, and whether the attacker is visible. Upon a successful parry, it displays appropriate messages, possibly triggers a riposte if the victim can use such skills, and applies defensive healing effects like Montrey's Grace. The function also updates skill improvement chances and handles special effects for NPCs and players. It returns true if the parry occurs, otherwise false.

params.ch: Pointer to the attacking character initiating the attack.
params.victim: Pointer to the character attempting to parry the attack.
params.attack_skill: The skill type used in the attack, influencing damage message selection.

('fight_8cc', 'bool check_parry(Characte.returns: Boolean value indicating whether the parry was successful (true) or not (false).

('fight_8cc', 'bool check_dual_parry(Cha.name: check_dual_parry
('fight_8cc', 'bool check_dual_parry(Cha.brief: Checks for a successful dual-wield parry from the off-hand and handles associated combat effects.
('fight_8cc', 'bool check_dual_parry(Cha.details: This function determines whether a character successfully parries an attack using their off-hand weapon, considering various conditions such as paralysis effects, skill levels, equipment, visibility, and character evolution stages. It calculates the chance of parry based on skills and weapon types, performs the roll, and if successful, displays appropriate combat messages. It also handles special counterattacks like hilt strikes, including dodge, blur, and shield block checks, and applies damage and healing effects accordingly. The function updates skill improvements and manages visual effects, providing a comprehensive response to dual parry attempts during combat.

params.ch: Pointer to the attacking Character performing the attack.
params.victim: Pointer to the defending Character attempting to parry with the off-hand weapon.
params.attack_skill: The skill::type representing the attack's skill type, used for damage message lookup.

('fight_8cc', 'bool check_dual_parry(Cha.returns: Boolean value indicating whether the parry was successful (true) or not (false).

('fight_8cc', 'void do_riposte(Character.name: do_riposte
('fight_8cc', 'void do_riposte(Character.brief: Performs a riposte counterattack if the victim successfully reacts to an attack.
('fight_8cc', 'void do_riposte(Character.details: This function initiates a riposte maneuver for the victim character in response to an attack from another character. It calculates the chance of riposting based on the victim's riposte skill level and level difference with the attacker. If the chance check succeeds, it further verifies whether the victim can dodge, blur, block with a shield, or parry the attack. If all checks pass, the victim executes a riposte, striking back at the attacker with a single attack, and skill improvement is attempted based on success. The function does not return a value but performs side effects such as message dispatching and combat actions.

params.victim: Pointer to the Character who is attempting to riposte.
params.ch: Pointer to the Character who initiated the attack and to whom the riposte is directed.

('fight_8cc', 'void do_riposte(Character.returns: Void; the function performs the riposte action and related effects without returning a value.

('fight_8cc', 'void dam_message(Characte.name: dam_message
('fight_8cc', 'void dam_message(Characte.argsstring: (Character *ch, Character *victim, int dam, skill::type attack_skill, int attack_type, bool immune, bool sanc_immune)
('fight_8cc', 'void dam_message(Characte.brief: Generates and dispatches an appropriate damage message to characters based on damage amount, attack type, and immunity status.
('fight_8cc', 'void dam_message(Characte.details: This function constructs and sends contextual damage messages to the attacker, victim, and room occupants, reflecting the damage dealt, attack type, and immunity conditions. It selects a damage descriptor string based on the damage amount, determines the attack noun from either a specified skill or attack table, and formats messages accordingly. It handles special cases such as immunity and sanctuary immunity, providing appropriate messages for each. The function also sets text colors for the attacker and victim during message dispatching. It does not return a value but performs side effects by sending messages to relevant characters and room.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character initiating the attack or damage source.
params.victim: Pointer to the Character receiving the damage.
params.dam: Integer representing the amount of damage dealt.
params.attack_skill: Skill::type indicating the specific skill used for damage, if known.
params.immune: Boolean indicating whether the victim is immune to the attack.
params.sanc_immune: Boolean indicating whether the victim's sanctuary immunity applies.

('fight_8cc', 'void dam_message(Characte.returns: This function has no return value; it performs message dispatching as a side effect.

('fight_8cc', 'void death_cry(Character.name: death_cry
('fight_8cc', 'void death_cry(Character.brief: Handles the death cry event for a character, including messaging and optional object creation.
('fight_8cc', 'void death_cry(Character.details: This function generates a death cry message for the specified character, potentially creates a related object (such as guts, head, or limbs) based on random selection and character's body parts, and broadcasts appropriate messages to surrounding rooms. It also manages object creation, formatting, and placement within the game world, and ensures messages are sent to adjacent rooms to simulate a realistic death event.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character object who has died, triggering the death cry and related effects.

('fight_8cc', 'void death_cry(Character.returns: Void; the function performs side effects such as messaging, object creation, and room notifications without returning a value.

('fight_8cc', 'int group_gain(Character.name: group_gain
('fight_8cc', 'int group_gain(Character.brief: Calculates and distributes experience points among group members after defeating a victim, adjusting their levels and handling related effects.
('fight_8cc', 'int group_gain(Character.details: This function computes experience points (XP) for each member of the group involved in defeating a victim, considering group composition, levels, and class variations. It updates each character's XP, handles level-up notifications, and manages item alignment restrictions. It also tracks the lowest XP awarded within the group for potential further processing. The function accounts for special room sectors and quest-related conditions, ensuring XP is only awarded under appropriate circumstances.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character initiating the group gain calculation, typically the attacker.
params.victim: Pointer to the Character that was defeated, whose death triggers XP distribution.

('fight_8cc', 'int group_gain(Character.returns: Returns an integer representing the lowest experience points awarded to any group member during this gain calculation.

('fight_8cc', 'int xp_compute(Character.name: xp_compute
('fight_8cc', 'int xp_compute(Character.brief: Calculates experience points (XP) for a character after a kill and adjusts the killer's alignment.
('fight_8cc', 'int xp_compute(Character.details: This function computes the experience points (XP) awarded to a character (gch) for defeating a victim character. The XP calculation is influenced by the level difference between the characters, the character's remort status, and various remort affect flags. Additionally, the function adjusts the killer's alignment based on the victim's alignment and other conditions. The function also applies modifiers based on the character's age, remort affects, and group dynamics, ensuring a balanced XP distribution among group members.

params.gch: A pointer to the Character object representing the killer whose XP and alignment are being calculated and adjusted.
params.victim: A pointer to the Character object representing the defeated character.
params.total_levels: The total levels of all characters in the group, used for XP distribution.
params.diff_classes: The number of different classes in the group, used to calculate group XP bonuses.

('fight_8cc', 'int xp_compute(Character.returns: The calculated experience points (XP) awarded to the character for the kill.

('fight_8cc', 'bool is_safe(Character *c.name: is_safe
('fight_8cc', 'bool is_safe(Character *c.brief: Determines whether an attack from one character to another is considered safe according to game rules.
('fight_8cc', 'bool is_safe(Character *c.details:
Determines if a character is in a safe location where combat is restricted.
This function evaluates if it is safe for 'ch' to attack 'victim' based on their current locations and game state. It checks if either character is in a null room, if the victim is in a safe room, in an arena, or in a quest area with PvP enabled. If any of these conditions are met, it returns true (attack is unsafe or disallowed). Otherwise, it delegates the safety check to 'is_safe_char', which applies additional game-specific safety rules. If 'showmsg' is true, it may display messages to 'ch' when an attack is disallowed due to safe room conditions.

params.ch: A pointer to the Character object representing the potential attacker.
params.victim: A pointer to the Character object representing the potential victim.
params.showmsg: A boolean indicating whether to display a message to the attacker if the location is safe.

('fight_8cc', 'bool is_safe(Character *c.returns: Returns true if the location is considered safe and combat is restricted; otherwise, returns false.

('fight_8cc', 'void make_corpse(Characte.name: make_corpse
('fight_8cc', 'void make_corpse(Characte.brief: Creates a corpse object from a character, transferring relevant attributes and items, and places it in the game world.
('fight_8cc', 'void make_corpse(Characte.details: This function generates a corpse object based on whether the character is an NPC or a player. It initializes the corpse with appropriate timers, ownership, and description, and transfers the character's inventory items to the corpse, handling special item types and decay timers. It also removes the character's equipped weapon before creating the corpse. The corpse is then placed in a location determined by the character's war status, level, or default morgue room. The function ensures proper cleanup and item handling to simulate death in the game environment.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character object that is to be turned into a corpse.

('fight_8cc', 'void make_corpse(Characte.returns: Void; the function performs side effects such as creating and placing a corpse object in the game world.

('fight_8cc', 'bool check_pulse(Characte.name: check_pulse
('fight_8cc', 'bool check_pulse(Characte.brief: Checks and updates a character's status during a pulse, handling death, incapacitation, and recovery scenarios.
('fight_8cc', 'bool check_pulse(Characte.details: This function evaluates the current health and status of the specified victim character during each pulse. It ensures that immortal characters with negative health are restored to minimal health, provides a chance for characters with the 'Die Hard' skill to recover some health if they are dying, updates their position accordingly, and outputs appropriate messages based on their new status. The function returns true if the character is still alive or recoverable, and false if the character has died. It also manages messaging to the character and the room, and performs skill improvement checks where applicable.

params.victim: Pointer to the Character object whose status is being checked and potentially updated.

('fight_8cc', 'bool check_pulse(Characte.returns: A boolean value indicating whether the character is still alive or recoverable (true), or has died (false).

('fight_8cc', 'void kill_off(Character *.name: kill_off
('fight_8cc', 'void kill_off(Character *.brief: Handles the death of a character, including logging, experience distribution, corpse management, and kill statistics.
('fight_8cc', 'void kill_off(Character *.details:
Handles the death of a character, including logging, experience distribution, corpse creation, looting, and updating kill/death statistics.
This function manages the process when a character (victim) is killed by another character (ch). It logs the death event, broadcasts notifications, awards experience points, handles corpse looting and auto-sacrifice, updates kill counts and war status, and performs cleanup such as removing the victim from the game world. Special considerations are made for NPC and player characters, including experience adjustments and arena-related rewards. The function also manages the creation of loot and handles potential suicide cases.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character object performing the kill or causing death.
params.victim: Pointer to the Character object who is being killed.

('fight_8cc', 'void kill_off(Character *.returns: Void; the function performs side effects such as logging, experience distribution, corpse handling, and updating character statistics, without returning a value.

('fight_8cc', 'void one_hit(Character *c.name: one_hit
('fight_8cc', 'void one_hit(Character *c.brief: Performs a single attack action from one character to another, calculating hit chance, damage, and applying special effects based on weapons and skills.
('fight_8cc', 'void one_hit(Character *c.details: This function executes a single combat hit from the attacker (ch) to the victim, considering weapon type, attack skill, character attributes, and situational modifiers. It determines attack type, calculates hit probability using THAC0 and armor class, and if successful, computes damage with weapon dice, skills, and bonuses. It also handles special weapon effects, such as poison or elemental damage, and applies additional spell effects like blindness or curses based on weapon attributes and attack context. The function manages different attack modes, including shadow form and riposte, and includes chance-based effects like berserker strikes. It ensures proper validation of characters' states and positions before executing the attack, and logs or applies effects accordingly.

params.ch: Pointer to the attacking Character initiating the hit.
params.victim: Pointer to the target Character being attacked.
params.attack_skill: The skill::type representing the attack type or skill used.
params.secondary: Boolean indicating if the attack is from the secondary weapon slot.

('fight_8cc', 'void one_hit(Character *c.returns: Void; the function performs the attack and applies effects without returning a value.

('fight_8cc', 'void mob_hit(Character *c.name: mob_hit
('fight_8cc', 'void mob_hit(Character *c.brief: Handles attack actions for a mobile character, including single and multiple strikes, special attacks, and combat maneuvers.
('fight_8cc', 'void mob_hit(Character *c.details: This function orchestrates the attack behavior of a non-player character (mob) during combat. It initiates a primary attack, potentially performs a secondary attack if conditions are met, executes area attacks if enabled, and applies effects such as haste or fast flags. It also randomly selects and performs special combat maneuvers like bash, berserk, disarm, kick, dirt kick, trip, or crush, depending on the mob's offensive flags. The function ensures that attack sequences respect combat state, character conditions, and skill levels, and it prevents infinite loops by checking attack skill types. It interacts with other combat-related functions to perform hits, apply effects, and manage combat state.

params.ch: Pointer to the attacking Character (mob) performing the attack sequence.
params.victim: Pointer to the target Character being attacked.
params.attack_skill: The skill::type representing the primary attack skill used in this attack cycle.

('fight_8cc', 'void mob_hit(Character *c.returns: Void; the function performs attack actions and side effects without returning a value.

('fight_8cc', 'void raw_kill(Character *.name: raw_kill
('fight_8cc', 'void raw_kill(Character *.brief: Handles the death of a character by stopping combat, triggering death effects, creating a corpse if applicable, and performing cleanup and saving operations.
('fight_8cc', 'void raw_kill(Character *.details: This function manages the process of killing a character in the game. It first stops any fighting involving the victim and triggers death-related mob programs. If the victim is marked as garbage, the function exits early. Otherwise, it checks the victim's location; if not in an arena, quest area, or duel room, it creates a corpse object. For NPCs, it increments their kill count and fully extracts them from the game. For player characters, it resets their PK timer, removes all affects, and depending on their location, either fully extracts them or relocates them to an altar room, resetting their armor. It then resets the victim's position and health/mana/stamina to minimal values, saves their character data, and saves ground items. The function performs side effects such as removing the character from the game world, creating corpses, and updating persistent data, but does not return a value.

params.victim: Pointer to the Character object representing the character to be killed.

('fight_8cc', 'void raw_kill(Character *.returns: Void; the function performs side effects such as removing the character, creating a corpse, and saving data, without returning a value.

('fight_8cc', 'void set_fighting(Charact.name: set_fighting
('fight_8cc', 'void set_fighting(Charact.details: This function sets a character to start fighting a specified victim. If the character is already engaged in combat, it logs a bug and exits. If the character is affected by sleep, the sleep affect is removed. The character's fighting target is set to the victim. If the character is not waiting and is not standing, their position is set to standing. If the character's starting position is flying and they can fly, their position is set to flying.

params.ch: A pointer to the Character object that is initiating combat.
params.victim: A pointer to the Character object that is the target of the combat.

('fight_8cc', 'void set_fighting(Charact.returns: This function does not return a value.

('fight_8cc', 'void combat_regen(Charact.name: combat_regen
('fight_8cc', 'void combat_regen(Charact.brief: Performs regeneration and damage effects on a character during combat, including health, mana, and stamina recovery, as well as environmental damage for vampires.
('fight_8cc', 'void combat_regen(Charact.details: This function updates the character's health, mana, and stamina based on various conditions, effects, and attributes. It handles special cases such as vampire exposure to sunlight, remort effects like mage and vampire regeneration, class-based bonuses, and active affects like regeneration and divine regeneration. For vampires, it applies sunlight damage or regeneration depending on the room's lighting and environment. It also grants bonus regeneration for characters with specific remort affects or class attributes, and adjusts the character's current hit points, mana, and stamina accordingly, ensuring they do not exceed their maximum values.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character object to be processed for regeneration and damage effects.

('fight_8cc', 'void combat_regen(Charact.returns: Void; the function modifies the character's hit points, mana, and stamina directly based on the regeneration logic.

('fight_8cc', 'void noncombat_regen(Char.name: noncombat_regen
('fight_8cc', 'void noncombat_regen(Char.brief: Regenerates a character's hit points, mana, and stamina when not in combat.
('fight_8cc', 'void noncombat_regen(Char.details: The noncombat_regen function calculates and applies regeneration to a character's hit points, mana, and stamina when they are not in combat. It increases each attribute by 10% of its maximum value, ensuring that none exceed their respective maximums. If all attributes are fully regenerated and the character is not in combat, a message is sent to the character indicating full recovery, and the combat timer is reset.

params.ch: A pointer to the Character object whose attributes are being regenerated.

('fight_8cc', 'void noncombat_regen(Char.returns: This function does not return a value.

('fight_8cc', 'void do_lay_on_hands(Char.name: do_lay_on_hands
('fight_8cc', 'void do_lay_on_hands(Char.brief: Executes the 'lay on hands' ability for a character.
('fight_8cc', 'void do_lay_on_hands(Char.details: This function allows a character to perform the 'lay on hands' ability, which typically involves healing or providing some beneficial effect to the target specified in the argument. The function takes a character object and a string argument that specifies the target or additional parameters for the ability.

params.ch: A pointer to the Character object that is performing the 'lay on hands' ability.
params.argument: A constant character pointer representing the target or additional parameters for the ability.

('fight_8cc', 'void do_lay_on_hands(Char.returns: This function does not return a value.

('fight_8cc', 'bool defense_heal(Charact.name: defense_heal
('fight_8cc', 'bool defense_heal(Charact.brief: Applies a defensive heal to a character based on a chance and percentage.
('fight_8cc', 'bool defense_heal(Charact.details: The function attempts to heal a character by a specified percentage of their maximum hit points, mana, and stamina. The healing occurs only if a random chance check, determined by the 'chance' parameter, is successful. This function is primarily designed for use with Montrey's Grace 5-piece set bonus but can be adapted for other skills or bonuses. The healing amount is calculated as a percentage of the character's maximum attributes.

params.victim: A pointer to the Character object that is the target of the defensive heal.
params.chance: An integer representing the percentage chance that the heal will occur.
params.percent: An integer representing the percentage of the character's maximum attributes to heal.

('fight_8cc', 'bool defense_heal(Charact.returns: Returns true if the heal is successfully applied, otherwise returns false.

('fight_8cc', 'void violence_update(void.name: violence_update
('fight_8cc', 'void violence_update(void.brief: Updates combat states, assists, and behaviors for all characters in the game world during each violence cycle.
('fight_8cc', 'void violence_update(void.details:
Manages ongoing combat activities, including assistance, regeneration, fleeing, and triggering mob programs, for all characters periodically.
This function iterates through all characters in the game world, ensuring combat assistance is checked, attributes are updated, and combat-related actions are processed. It manages NPC hunting behaviors, handles regeneration during combat and non-combat states, manages character standing up after being knocked down, executes attack sequences, and triggers mob programs related to fighting. Additionally, it handles fleeing logic based on health and status effects, switches attack focus for charmed NPCs, and performs necessary cleanup when characters are no longer fighting. The function is called periodically to simulate ongoing combat and character interactions within the game environment.

params.void: No parameters are passed; the function operates on the global game state and character list.

('fight_8cc', 'void violence_update(void.returns: This function has no return value; it performs side effects such as updating character states, initiating combat, and triggering events.

('fight_8cc', 'void check_protection_aur.name: check_protection_aura
('fight_8cc', 'void check_protection_aur.brief: Checks and applies protection aura effects on a victim based on the attacker's alignment and aura properties.
('fight_8cc', 'void check_protection_aur.details: This function evaluates whether the attacker character (ch), who is either evil or good, has an active protection aura (protection_evil or protection_good) on the victim character. If such an aura exists and its 'evolution' level meets certain thresholds, the function may trigger visual and gameplay effects such as shock or stun, with probabilities influenced by the aura's evolution level. The function also manages combat states like daze or wait, and dispatches contextual messages to involved characters to reflect the aura's impact.

params.ch: Pointer to the attacking Character object, used to determine alignment and trigger effects.
params.victim: Pointer to the Character object being protected or affected by the aura.

('fight_8cc', 'void check_protection_aur.returns: Void; the function performs side effects including state changes and message dispatching without returning a value.

('fight_8cc', 'void multi_hit(Character.name: multi_hit
('fight_8cc', 'void multi_hit(Character.brief: Performs multiple attack actions for a character during combat, including handling dual-wielding, special attacks, and combat effects.
('fight_8cc', 'void multi_hit(Character.details:
Executes multiple attack hits for a character against a victim, considering skills, effects, and special conditions.
This function manages the attack sequence for a character ('ch') against a victim ('victim') using a specified attack skill ('attack_skill'). It resets the combat timer if the character is not an NPC, executes an initial attack, and then performs additional attacks based on character skills, effects, and equipment, such as dual-wielding, haste, slow, and special remort effects. It also checks for combat-related effects like clumsiness and weapon dropping due to remort abilities. The function dispatches attack messages, applies effects, and updates combat state accordingly, but does not return a value.

params.ch: Pointer to the attacking Character initiating the attack sequence.
params.victim: Pointer to the target Character being attacked.
params.attack_skill: The skill::type representing the attack type or skill used in the attack sequence.

('fight_8cc', 'void multi_hit(Character.returns: Void; performs attack actions and side effects without returning a value.

('fight_8cc', 'int affect_callback_weake.name: affect_callback_weaken_bonewall
('fight_8cc', 'int affect_callback_weake.brief: Handles the weakening of a bone wall affect upon a hit.
('fight_8cc', 'int affect_callback_weake.details: This function is invoked when a bone wall affect is hit. It checks if the affect type is 'bone_wall'. If so, it decreases the duration of the affect by 1, ensuring it does not go below zero. Additionally, if the affect's level is greater than 5, it decrements the level by 1. The function ensures that only one bone wall affect is processed by returning 1.

params.node: A pointer to an affect::Affect object representing the affect to be processed.
params.null: A placeholder parameter that is not used in this function.

('fight_8cc', 'int affect_callback_weake.returns: Returns 1 to indicate that the processing of the bone wall affect should stop after one affect.

('fight_8cc', 'bool damage(Character *ch.name: damage
('fight_8cc', 'bool damage(Character *ch.argsstring: (Character *ch, Character *victim, int dam, skill::type attack_skill, int attack_type, int dam_type, bool show, bool spell)
('fight_8cc', 'bool damage(Character *ch.brief: Calculates and applies damage from an attack to a victim, considering various effects, defenses, and messaging.
('fight_8cc', 'bool damage(Character *ch.details:
Inflicts damage from a hit on a victim, applying various modifiers, effects, and messaging.
This function handles damage calculation and application in combat scenarios. It accounts for spell effects, damage modifications based on character attributes, armor, and special effects like sanctuary, barrier, and elemental auras. It also manages defensive checks such as dodge, parry, shield block, and blur, and applies damage reductions from various effects. The function updates the victim's hit points, triggers death if necessary, and sends appropriate damage messages. It also handles special cases like healing effects from divine attributes and link-dead character handling. The function performs side effects such as logging, messaging, and state updates, and returns a boolean indicating whether the victim survived or was killed.

params.ch: Pointer to the attacking or damaging Character object.
params.victim: Pointer to the Character object receiving damage.
params.dam: Integer amount of damage to be inflicted.
params.attack_skill: Skill type used for the attack, influencing damage and effects.
params.dam_type: Damage type, used for resistances and effects.
params.show: Boolean indicating whether to display damage messages.
params.spell: Boolean indicating if the damage is from a spell, affecting damage calculations.

('fight_8cc', 'bool damage(Character *ch.returns: Boolean indicating whether the damage resulted in the victim's death or incapacitation (false if dead, true otherwise).

('fight_8cc', 'bool is_safe_char(Charact.name: is_safe_char
('fight_8cc', 'bool is_safe_char(Charact.brief: Determines whether it is safe for a character to attack a victim based on game rules and context.
('fight_8cc', 'bool is_safe_char(Charact.details:
Determines whether a character is safe to attack or engage with, considering game rules and context.
This function evaluates various conditions to decide if 'ch' can safely attack 'victim'. It considers immortality status, current fighting state, NPC and player-specific restrictions, ownership and charm effects, and player-kill flags. It also prevents attacking shopkeepers, trainers, healers, pets, and charmed creatures unless appropriate conditions are met. The function optionally displays messages to 'ch' explaining why an attack is unsafe. It returns true if the attack is unsafe (should be prevented), and false if it is safe to proceed.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character initiating the action, used to determine attacker status and messaging.
params.victim: Pointer to the Character being targeted, whose safety status is evaluated.
params.showmsg: Boolean flag indicating whether to display a message to 'ch' when the attack is disallowed.

('fight_8cc', 'bool is_safe_char(Charact.returns: Returns true if the attack is considered unsafe (i.e., the action should be prevented), and false if it is safe to proceed.

('fight_8cc', 'bool is_safe_spell(Charac.name: is_safe_spell
('fight_8cc', 'bool is_safe_spell(Charac.brief: Determines if casting a spell on a target character is safe.
('fight_8cc', 'bool is_safe_spell(Charac.details:
Determines if casting a spell on a target is safe.
This function evaluates various conditions to determine if it is safe for a character (ch) to cast a spell on a target character (victim). It considers factors such as the room's safety status, the characters' roles (NPC or player), their group affiliations, and specific game mechanics like invisibility levels, charm effects, and ongoing battles. The function is designed to prevent illegal or unintended interactions, such as attacking in safe zones or targeting non-opponents in a duel arena.

params.ch: A pointer to the Character object representing the caster of the spell.
params.victim: A pointer to the Character object representing the target of the spell.
params.area: A boolean indicating if the spell affects an area rather than a single target.

('fight_8cc', 'bool is_safe_spell(Charac.returns: Returns true if casting the spell is considered safe according to the game's rules, otherwise returns false.

('fight_8cc', 'void update_pos(Character.name: update_pos
('fight_8cc', 'void update_pos(Character.brief: Updates the position of a character based on their hit points.
('fight_8cc', 'void update_pos(Character.details:
Updates the position of a character based on their current hit points.
This function adjusts the position of a character ('victim') according to their current hit points. If the character's hit points are greater than zero, their position is set to standing if they are currently stunned or worse. If the character is an NPC and their hit points are less than one, or if their hit points are less than or equal to -11, their position is set to dead. For hit points between -10 and -6, the position is set to mortal. For hit points between -5 and -3, the position is set to incapacitated. Otherwise, the position is set to stunned.

params.victim: A pointer to the Character object whose position is to be updated based on their hit points.

('fight_8cc', 'void update_pos(Character.returns: This function does not return a value.

('fight_8cc', 'void stop_fighting(Charac.name: stop_fighting
('fight_8cc', 'void stop_fighting(Charac.brief: Stops a character from fighting and updates their position.
('fight_8cc', 'void stop_fighting(Charac.details:
Stops the fighting for a character and optionally for their opponent.
This function iterates over all characters in the game world and stops the specified character, 'ch', from fighting. If 'fBoth' is true, it also stops any character currently fighting 'ch'. The fighting status is cleared, and the character's position is reset based on whether they are an NPC or not. The position is updated using the 'update_pos' function.

params.ch: A pointer to the Character object whose fighting status is to be stopped.
params.fBoth: A boolean flag indicating whether to stop the fighting for both the specified character and their opponent.

('fight_8cc', 'void stop_fighting(Charac.returns: This function does not return a value.

('fight_8cc', 'void do_berserk(Character.name: do_berserk
('fight_8cc', 'void do_berserk(Character.brief: Initiates a berserk state for a character if conditions are met, with success depending on skill level and randomness.
('fight_8cc', 'void do_berserk(Character.details: This function attempts to activate a berserk effect for the specified character. It first checks if the character has sufficient skill level in berserk; if not, it outputs a message and exits. It then verifies whether the character is already affected by berserk, frenzy, or calm states, preventing reapplication if so. The function also ensures the character is not in a calm state. It deducts stamina for the action, and if successful, calculates a chance of success influenced by fighting status and current hit points percentage. A random percentile determines if the berserk effect is triggered. On success, the character's hit points are slightly restored, a message is sent, and the berserk affect is applied with duration and evolution level based on character level and skill. On failure, the character's wait state is increased, and a message indicates no effect. The function also calls skill improvement checks based on success or failure.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character initiating the berserk action.
params.argument: Additional command arguments (not used in this implementation).

('fight_8cc', 'void do_berserk(Character.returns: Void; the function performs side effects such as messaging, affect application, and state changes without returning a value.

('fight_8cc', 'void do_bash(Character *c.name: do_bash
('fight_8cc', 'void do_bash(Character *c.brief: Performs a bash attack from a character against a target, with success, damage, and knockdown mechanics.
('fight_8cc', 'void do_bash(Character *c.details: This function enables a character to execute a bash attack on a specified target or the current fighting opponent. It checks skill levels, combat states, safety conditions, and whether the target is valid and reachable. The function calculates the chance of a successful bash based on character attributes, skill evolution, and other modifiers, then determines if the attack hits, misses, or results in a knockdown. Successful knocks cause the victim to be dazed and knocked to the ground, inflicting damage and applying appropriate states. The function also manages combat engagement, stamina deduction, and skill improvement checks, with messaging to involved characters. It handles various edge cases such as fighting status, safe zones, and special effects like charm or group considerations.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character executing the bash action.
params.argument: String containing the target's name or empty to default to current fighting opponent.

('fight_8cc', 'void do_bash(Character *c.returns: Void; the function performs side effects such as messaging, state changes, and damage application without returning a value.

('fight_8cc', 'void do_dirt(Character *c.name: do_dirt
('fight_8cc', 'void do_dirt(Character *c.brief: Performs the 'dirt kick' action, attempting to blind an opponent with dirt.
('fight_8cc', 'void do_dirt(Character *c.details: This function allows a character to attempt to blind a target by kicking dirt into their eyes. It first checks the character's skill level in dirt kicking; if zero, it simply informs the character they get their feet dirty. It then determines the target: if no argument is provided, it defaults to the current fighting opponent; otherwise, it searches for a character in the same room matching the argument. The function verifies that the target is not already blinded, that the character is not flying, and that the target is not the character themselves. It also checks for safe locations and kill-stealing conditions, and ensures the character is not charmed to the victim. The chance to blind depends on the character's dexterity, speed effects, level difference, and terrain. If successful, it applies a blindness effect and inflicts minor damage; otherwise, it reports a miss. The function manages skill improvement, stamina deduction, and action delays accordingly.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character executing the dirt kick action.
params.argument: String containing the target's name or entity, used to identify the victim.

('fight_8cc', 'void do_dirt(Character *c.returns: Void; the function performs side effects such as messaging, applying effects, and damage, without returning a value.

('fight_8cc', 'bool trip(Character *ch,.name: trip
('fight_8cc', 'bool trip(Character *ch,.brief: Attempts to trip a victim character, applying effects and damage if successful.
('fight_8cc', 'bool trip(Character *ch,.details: This function calculates the chance to trip a victim based on size differences, attributes, speed, and level differences between the attacker and victim. It modifies the chance based on various conditions such as haste effects and size, then performs a skill check against a random percentile. If successful, it dazes and knocks the victim down, inflicts bash damage, and updates the victim's state accordingly. It also checks and possibly improves the victim's 'standfast' skill if applicable. The function returns true if the trip succeeds, otherwise false, and performs side effects such as damage application and state changes.

params.ch: Pointer to the attacker Character performing the trip.
params.victim: Pointer to the victim Character being targeted for the trip.
params.chance: Initial chance percentage for the trip attempt, modified within the function.
params.attack_skill: The skill::type representing the attack skill used for damage calculation.

('fight_8cc', 'bool trip(Character *ch,.returns: Boolean value indicating whether the trip was successful (true) or not (false).

('fight_8cc', 'void do_trip(Character *c.name: do_trip
('fight_8cc', 'void do_trip(Character *c.brief: Performs a trip action where a character attempts to trip a target, applying effects and damage if successful.
('fight_8cc', 'void do_trip(Character *c.details: This function enables a character to attempt to trip either their current fighting opponent or a specified target in the same room. It checks for skill level, safety conditions, whether the target is already down or flying, and whether the character is charmed and linked to the target. It deducts stamina from the character, then performs the trip attempt, applying success or failure effects accordingly. Success results in the target falling and being incapacitated, with appropriate messages dispatched to involved parties. The function also manages waiting states based on skill beats and updates skill improvement chances.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character performing the trip action.
params.argument: String containing the optional target's name or identifier.

('fight_8cc', 'void do_trip(Character *c.returns: Void; the function performs side effects such as messaging, state changes, and skill improvements without returning a value.

('fight_8cc', 'bool check_attack_ok(Char.name: check_attack_ok
('fight_8cc', 'bool check_attack_ok(Char.brief: Checks whether a character can initiate an attack on a victim based on game rules and conditions.
('fight_8cc', 'bool check_attack_ok(Char.details: This function evaluates multiple game-specific conditions to determine if a character ('ch') is permitted to attack another character ('victim'). It verifies if the attack occurs in an arena that hasn't started, if the attacker is a morphed NPC attempting to attack a player or pet, if the attacker is under fear effects, if the attacker is attempting to attack themselves, if the location is safe, if the victim is already fighting someone else outside the attacker's group, or if the attacker is charmed and loyal to the victim. If any condition fails, it provides appropriate feedback messages and returns false; otherwise, it returns true, indicating the attack is permissible.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character initiating the attack.
params.victim: Pointer to the Character being targeted for attack.

('fight_8cc', 'bool check_attack_ok(Char.returns: Boolean value indicating whether the attack is allowed (true) or not (false).

('fight_8cc', 'void do_kill(Character *c.name: do_kill
('fight_8cc', 'void do_kill(Character *c.brief: Handles the command for a character to attack and attempt to kill another character in the game.
('fight_8cc', 'void do_kill(Character *c.details: This function processes the 'kill' command issued by a character, attempting to initiate combat with a specified target. It verifies input validity, checks for special skills like 'blind fight' to determine visibility constraints, locates the target character in the same room, and ensures attack conditions are met. If all checks pass, it sets the character's attack state, verifies if the attack is permissible, and proceeds to perform multiple attack hits against the target, also updating the character's fighting status. Side effects include messaging the character, updating combat states, and potentially setting killer flags.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character issuing the kill command.
params.argument: String containing the name or identifier of the target to attack.

('fight_8cc', 'void do_kill(Character *c.returns: Void; the function performs actions and side effects without returning a value.

('fight_8cc', 'void do_battle(Character.name: do_battle
('fight_8cc', 'void do_battle(Character.brief: Handles the arena battle commands, allowing immortals to start, stop, and check the status of battles, and players to join battles within specified level ranges.
('fight_8cc', 'void do_battle(Character.details: This function processes the 'battle' command issued by characters, with behavior differing based on whether the character is an immortal or a player. Immortals can start, stop, and check the status of arena battles, setting parameters such as level range and entry fee, and broadcasting updates. Players can join ongoing battles if they meet level requirements, are not already in combat or arena, and can afford the fee. The function manages arena state variables, teleports players into the arena, and announces battle events. It also performs validation checks on input arguments, arena occupancy, and character status to ensure proper operation.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character issuing the command, used to determine permissions, context, and to send responses.
params.argument: String containing additional command parameters, such as subcommands ('start', 'stop', 'status') or level and fee specifications.

('fight_8cc', 'void do_sing(Character *c.brief: Performs the singing action by a character, attempting to charm a target character if conditions are met.
('fight_8cc', 'void do_sing(Character *c.details: This function allows a character to sing a melody, optionally directed at a specified target. It checks for valid target presence, safety conditions, and room restrictions. If the singing is successful based on skill level, attributes, and chance calculations, it establishes a charm effect on the target, making them follow and be influenced by the singer. The function also handles messaging and follower relationships, and updates the charm affect on the victim.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character performing the singing action.
params.argument: String specifying the target character to sing to; if empty, the singer performs solo.

('fight_8cc', 'void do_sing(Character *c.returns: Void; the function performs side effects such as messaging, follower management, and applying effects without returning a value.

It checks for skill proficiency, target presence, safety conditions, weapon wielded, and whether the target is hurt or suspicious. It also verifies morphed player restrictions and stamina availability. If conditions are met, it executes the attack, possibly inflicting paralysis if the character's evolution level is high enough. The function handles NPC and player characters differently, with NPCs performing an automatic hit and players requiring skill checks and success rolls. It also manages waiting states and skill improvement chances.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character initiating the backstab.

('fight_8cc', 'void do_backstab(Characte.returns: Void; the function performs side effects such as messaging, damage application, and state changes without returning a value.

('fight_8cc', 'void do_shadow(Character.name: do_shadow
('fight_8cc', 'void do_shadow(Character.brief: Implements the 'shadow form' remort skill allowing a character to enter a shadow state during combat.
('fight_8cc', 'void do_shadow(Character.details: This function enables a character to activate the shadow form skill if they meet specific conditions: possessing the skill, being in combat, wielding a weapon, and not having an existing shadow form effect on the target. It optionally targets an opponent the character is fighting, verifies the opponent's presence and engagement, and then attempts to apply the shadow form effect. The function also deducts stamina, manages wait states, and updates the target's status with the shadow form effect.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character executing the shadow form skill.
params.argument: String containing optional target information or additional command arguments.

('fight_8cc', 'void do_shadow(Character.returns: Void; performs actions such as attacking, applying effects, and messaging without returning a value.

('fight_8cc', 'void do_circle(Character.name: do_circle
('fight_8cc', 'void do_circle(Character.brief: Performs a circle attack maneuver by a character against a fighting opponent, with success determined by skill level and chance.
('fight_8cc', 'void do_circle(Character.details: This function enables a character to execute a 'circle' attack if they possess the skill and are currently engaged in combat. It first checks if the character has the 'circle' skill; if not, it displays a humorous message. It then verifies that the character is fighting; if not, it prompts the character to engage in combat. The function processes an optional argument to identify a specific opponent; if provided, it confirms that the opponent is present and currently fighting the character's target, and that both are in the same group. It also ensures the character is wielding a weapon before proceeding. The function then checks if the character is a killer and deducts stamina accordingly. It sets the character's wait state based on the skill's beats. A success chance is calculated based on the character's skill level; if successful, it performs multiple attack hits on the opponent; otherwise, it displays a miss message and inflicts no damage. The function also handles skill improvement attempts based on success or failure.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character executing the circle attack.
params.argument: String containing optional target specification or additional command arguments.

('fight_8cc', 'void do_circle(Character.returns: Void; the function performs attack actions, messaging, and state changes without returning a value.

('fight_8cc', 'void do_flee(Character *c.name: do_flee
('fight_8cc', 'void do_flee(Character *c.brief: Handles the fleeing action for a character attempting to escape combat by moving to an adjacent room.
('fight_8cc', 'void do_flee(Character *c.details: This function enables a character to attempt to flee from combat within the game environment. It first checks if the character is currently fighting and standing; if not, it aborts. It calculates the chance to flee based on the character's attributes (dexterity and wisdom), weight carried, and the presence of opponents, adjusting the probability accordingly. It then evaluates available exits, considering visibility and door states, and attempts to move the character through a randomly selected exit based on the calculated chance. Successful fleeing results in stopping combat, updating the character's location, and possibly deducting experience points depending on the character's guild. If the fleeing character is an NPC with aggressive behavior, it may set hunting targets for nearby aggressive NPCs. If no exit is successfully taken, the character remains in place with a message indicating failure.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character object attempting to flee.
params.argument: String argument provided to the command, not used directly in this implementation.

('fight_8cc', 'void do_flee(Character *c.returns: Void; the function performs side effects such as moving the character, updating combat states, and sending messages, without returning a value.

('fight_8cc', 'void do_rescue(Character.name: do_rescue
('fight_8cc', 'void do_rescue(Character.brief: Handles the rescue action where a character attempts to save a teammate from an attacker.
('fight_8cc', 'void do_rescue(Character.details: This function processes a rescue command issued by a character, attempting to rescue a specified victim present in the same room. It verifies input validity, ensures both characters are in the same group, and that the victim is currently fighting. It then identifies the attacker fighting the victim, deducts stamina from the rescuer, and performs a skill check to determine success. On success, it interrupts the attacker's fight, initiates combat between the rescuer and attacker, and provides appropriate in-game messages. The function also updates fighting states and checks for potential kill flags. It performs side effects such as messaging, stamina deduction, and fight state management, without returning a value.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character initiating the rescue command.
params.argument: String containing the name or identifier of the victim to rescue.

('fight_8cc', 'void do_rescue(Character.returns: Void; the function performs side effects such as messaging, fight state updates, and skill checks, without returning a value.

('fight_8cc', 'void do_kick(Character *c.name: do_kick
('fight_8cc', 'void do_kick(Character *c.brief: Performs a kick attack from a character against their current fighting opponent, including damage calculation, potential additional effects, and skill improvement.
('fight_8cc', 'void do_kick(Character *c.details: This function initiates a kick attack for a character ('ch') against their current opponent ('victim') if they are fighting. It first checks if the character has the 'kick' skill; if not, it outputs a message and exits. It then verifies that the character is fighting; if not, it notifies the character and exits. The function deducts stamina for the kick skill, applies a wait state based on the skill's beats, and checks if the character is a killer. It then determines if the attack hits based on a percentage roll against the character's kick skill level. On a successful hit, it calculates damage based on the character's level, attributes, and skill, and applies it to the victim. If the victim is defeated or becomes garbage, the function exits. It also checks for skill improvement upon success. For characters with higher evolution levels, additional effects such as a second hit or a trip attempt are possible, with success chances depending on the evolution level and other conditions. If the attack misses, it deals zero damage and checks for skill improvement on failure.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character executing the kick attack.
params.argument: String containing additional command arguments (not used directly in this function).

('fight_8cc', 'void do_kick(Character *c.returns: Void; the function performs side effects such as damage application, messaging, and skill progression, but does not return a value.

('fight_8cc', 'void do_crush(Character *.name: do_crush
('fight_8cc', 'void do_crush(Character *.brief: Performs a crushing attack (grappling move) on the current fighting opponent if skilled and engaged in combat.
('fight_8cc', 'void do_crush(Character *.details: This function initiates a crushing attack by the character 'ch' on their current fighting target. It first checks if 'ch' possesses the 'crush' skill; if not, it outputs a message and exits. It then verifies that 'ch' is currently fighting; if not, it notifies 'ch' and exits. The function sets the character's wait state based on the 'crush' skill's beats value. It then performs a skill check: if successful, it inflicts damage within a range scaled by the character's level and calls 'check_improve' with success; otherwise, it inflicts zero damage and calls 'check_improve' with failure. Finally, it invokes 'check_killer' to determine if the attack qualifies as a killing blow for game logging and flagging purposes.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character performing the crush attack.
params.argument: String containing additional command arguments (not used in this implementation).

('fight_8cc', 'void do_crush(Character *.returns: Void; the function does not return a value but performs side effects such as damage application, messaging, and state updates.

('fight_8cc', 'void do_disarm(Character.brief: Performs a disarm attempt by a character against a target, with success depending on skills, conditions, and randomness.
('fight_8cc', 'void do_disarm(Character.details: This function enables a character to attempt to disarm their opponent during combat. It first checks if the character knows the disarm skill and identifies the victim either from current fighting or a specified target. It verifies safety conditions, visibility, and whether the character is fighting the victim directly or indirectly. The function considers the character's evolution level, blindness, and special effects like talon or noremove to determine disarm success chances. It calculates the chance based on skills, attributes, levels, and situational modifiers, then performs a random roll to decide success. On success, it disarms the victim's weapon, possibly knocking it into the current room or adjacent rooms, with different messages based on the evolution level. It also manages weapon removal, potential re-equipping, and applies wait states. If the disarm fails, appropriate messages are shown, and the character's wait state is set. The function also checks for blindness effects and skill improvements.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character performing the disarm attempt.
params.argument: String argument specifying the target; if empty, defaults to current fighting opponent.

('fight_8cc', 'void do_disarm(Character.returns: Void; the function performs side effects such as messaging, weapon disarming, and state updates without returning a value.

It first checks if an argument is provided; if not, it prompts for a target. It then searches for the target character in the room. If the target is not found or is the same as the attacker, it provides appropriate feedback. If the target is immortal, the attack is thwarted with a message indicating the target's immortality. Otherwise, it announces the slaying to the room, sends personalized messages to the attacker and victim, and if the victim is a player, broadcasts an announcement of their death. Finally, it calls 'raw_kill' to handle the victim's death, including cleanup and corpse creation.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character initiating the slay action.
params.argument: String containing the name or identifier of the target character to be slain.

On success, it initiates a combat wait state, reduces stamina, displays success messages, checks for skill improvement, and applies the 'hammerstrike' affect to the character. On failure, it imposes a longer wait state, reduces stamina more significantly, displays failure messages, and checks for skill improvement. The function modifies the character's state and outputs appropriate messages but does not return a value.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character executing the skill.
